name: S_ADD_U32
description: Add two unsigned inputs, store the result into a scalar register and store the carry-out bit into SCC.

name: S_SUB_U32
description: Subtract the second unsigned input from the first input, store the result into a scalar register and store the carry-out bit into SCC.

name: S_ADD_I32
description: Add two signed inputs, store the result into a scalar register and store the carry-out bit into SCC.
notes: This opcode is not suitable for use with S_ADDC_U32 for implementing 64-bit operations.

name: S_SUB_I32
description: Subtract the second signed input from the first input, store the result into a scalar register and store the carry- out bit into SCC.
notes: This opcode is not suitable for use with S_SUBB_U32 for implementing 64-bit operations.

name: S_ADDC_U32
description: Add two unsigned inputs and a carry-in bit, store the result into a scalar register and store the carry-out bit into SCC.

name: S_SUBB_U32
description: Subtract the second unsigned input from the first input, subtract the carry-in bit, store the result into a scalar register and store the carry-out bit into SCC.

name: S_ABSDIFF_I32
description: Calculate the absolute value of difference between two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
notes:
Functional examples:

S_ABSDIFF_I32(0x00000002, 0x00000005) => 0x00000003
S_ABSDIFF_I32(0xffffffff, 0x00000000) => 0x00000001
S_ABSDIFF_I32(0x80000000, 0x00000000) => 0x80000000          // Note: result is negative!
S_ABSDIFF_I32(0x80000000, 0x00000001) => 0x7fffffff
S_ABSDIFF_I32(0x80000000, 0xffffffff) => 0x7fffffff
S_ABSDIFF_I32(0x80000000, 0xfffffffe) => 0x7ffffffe

name: S_LSHL_B32
description: Given a shift count in the second scalar input, calculate the logical shift left of the first scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_LSHL_B64
description: Given a shift count in the second scalar input, calculate the logical shift left of the first scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_LSHR_B32
description: Given a shift count in the second scalar input, calculate the logical shift right of the first scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_LSHR_B64
description: Given a shift count in the second scalar input, calculate the logical shift right of the first scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_ASHR_I32
description: Given a shift count in the second scalar input, calculate the arithmetic shift right (preserving sign bit) of the first scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.i = 32'I(signext(S0.i) >> S1.u[4 : 0].u);
  SCC = D0.i != 0

name: S_ASHR_I64
description: Given a shift count in the second scalar input, calculate the arithmetic shift right (preserving sign bit) of the first scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_LSHL1_ADD_U32
description: Calculate the logical shift left of the first input by 1, then add the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the summation results in an unsigned overflow.

name: S_LSHL2_ADD_U32
description: Calculate the logical shift left of the first input by 2, then add the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the summation results in an unsigned overflow.

name: S_LSHL3_ADD_U32
description: Calculate the logical shift left of the first input by 3, then add the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the summation results in an unsigned overflow.

name: S_LSHL4_ADD_U32
description: Calculate the logical shift left of the first input by 4, then add the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the summation results in an unsigned overflow.

name: S_MIN_I32
description: Select the minimum of two signed integers, store the selected value into a scalar register and set SCC iff the first value is selected.
pseudocode:
SCC = S0.i < S1.i;
  D0.i = SCC ? S0.i : S1.i

name: S_MIN_U32
description: Select the minimum of two unsigned integers, store the selected value into a scalar register and set SCC iff the first value is selected.
pseudocode:
SCC = S0.u < S1.u;
  D0.u = SCC ? S0.u : S1.u

name: S_MAX_I32
description: Select the maximum of two signed integers, store the selected value into a scalar register and set SCC iff the first value is selected.
pseudocode:
SCC = S0.i > S1.i;
  D0.i = SCC ? S0.i : S1.i

name: S_MAX_U32
description: Select the maximum of two unsigned integers, store the selected value into a scalar register and set SCC iff the first value is selected.
pseudocode:
SCC = S0.u > S1.u;
  D0.u = SCC ? S0.u : S1.u

name: S_AND_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = (S0.u & S1.u);
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_AND_B64
description: Calculate bitwise AND on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_OR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise OR on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = (S0.u | S1.u);
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_OR_B64
description: Calculate bitwise OR on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_XOR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise XOR on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = (S0.u ^ S1.u);
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_XOR_B64
description: Calculate bitwise XOR on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_NAND_B32
description: Calculate bitwise NAND on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = ~(S0.u & S1.u);
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_NAND_B64
description: Calculate bitwise NAND on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.

name: S_NOR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise NOR on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = ~(S0.u | S1.u);
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_NOR_B64
description: Calculate bitwise NOR on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.

name: S_XNOR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise XNOR on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = ~(S0.u ^ S1.u);
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_XNOR_B64
description: Calculate bitwise XNOR on two scalar inputs, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.

name: S_AND_NOT1_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND with the first input and the negation of the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = (S0.u & ~S1.u);
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_AND_NOT1_B64
description: Calculate bitwise AND with the first input and the negation of the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.

name: S_OR_NOT1_B32
description: Calculate bitwise OR with the first input and the negation of the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = (S0.u | ~S1.u);
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_OR_NOT1_B64
description: Calculate bitwise OR with the first input and the negation of the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC if the result is nonzero.

name: S_BFE_U32
description: Extract an unsigned bitfield from the first input using field offset and size encoded in the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_BFE_I32
description: Extract a signed bitfield from the first input using field offset and size encoded in the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_BFE_U64
description: Extract an unsigned bitfield from the first input using field offset and size encoded in the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_BFE_I64
description: Extract a signed bitfield from the first input using field offset and size encoded in the second input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_BFM_B32
description: Calculate a bitfield mask given a field offset and size and store the result in a scalar register.

name: S_BFM_B64
description: Calculate a bitfield mask given a field offset and size and store the result in a scalar register.

name: S_MUL_I32
description: Multiply two signed integers and store the result into a scalar register.
pseudocode: D0.i = S0.i * S1.i

name: S_MUL_HI_U32
description: Multiply two unsigned integers and store the high 32 bits of the result into a scalar register.

name: S_MUL_HI_I32
description: Multiply two signed integers and store the high 32 bits of the result into a scalar register.

name: S_CSELECT_B32
description: Select the first input if SCC is true otherwise select the second input, then store the selected input into a scalar register.
pseudocode: D0.u = SCC ? S0.u : S1.u

name: S_CSELECT_B64
description: Select the first input if SCC is true otherwise select the second input, then store the selected input into a scalar register.

name: S_PACK_LL_B32_B16
description: Pack two 16-bit scalar values into a scalar register.

name: S_PACK_LH_B32_B16
description: Pack two 16-bit scalar values into a scalar register.

name: S_PACK_HH_B32_B16
description: Pack two 16-bit scalar values into a scalar register.

name: S_PACK_HL_B32_B16
description: Pack two 16-bit scalar values into a scalar register.

name: S_MOVK_I32
description: Sign extend a literal 16-bit constant and store the result into a scalar register.
pseudocode: D0.i = 32'I(signext(SIMM16.i16))

name: S_VERSION
description: Do nothing. This opcode is used to specify the microcode version for tools that interpret shader microcode. Argument is ignored by hardware. This opcode is not designed for inserting wait states as the next instruction may issue in the same cycle. Do not use this opcode to resolve wait state hazards, use S_NOP instead. This opcode may also be used to validate microcode is running with the correct compatibility settings in drivers and functional models that support multiple generations. We strongly encourage this opcode be included at the top of every shader block to simplify debug and catch configuration errors. This opcode must appear in the first 16 bytes of a block of shader code in order to be recognized by external tools and functional models. Avoid placing opcodes > 32 bits or encodings that are not available in all versions of the microcode before the S_VERSION opcode. If this opcode is absent then tools are allowed to make an educated guess of the microcode version using cues from the environment; the guess may be incorrect and lead to an invalid decode. It is highly recommended that this be the first opcode of a shader block except for trap handlers, where it should be the second opcode (allowing the first opcode to be a 32-bit branch to accommodate context switch). SIMM16[7:0] specifies the microcode version. SIMM16[15:8] must be set to zero.
pseudocode:
nop();
  // Do nothing - for use by tools only

name: S_CMOVK_I32
description: Move the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant into a scalar register iff SCC is nonzero.
pseudocode:
if SCC then

       D0.i = 32'I(signext(SIMM16.i16))
  endif

name: S_CMPK_EQ_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is equal to the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = 64'I(S0.i) == signext(SIMM16.i16)

name: S_CMPK_LG_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is less than or greater than the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = 64'I(S0.i) != signext(SIMM16.i16)

name: S_CMPK_GT_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is greater than the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = 64'I(S0.i) > signext(SIMM16.i16)

name: S_CMPK_GE_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is greater than or equal to the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = 64'I(S0.i) >= signext(SIMM16.i16)

name: S_CMPK_LT_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is less than the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = 64'I(S0.i) < signext(SIMM16.i16)

name: S_CMPK_LE_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is less than or equal to the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = 64'I(S0.i) <= signext(SIMM16.i16)

name: S_CMPK_EQ_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is equal to the zero extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u == 32'U(SIMM16.u16)

name: S_CMPK_LG_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is less than or greater than the zero extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u != 32'U(SIMM16.u16)

name: S_CMPK_GT_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is greater than the zero extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u > 32'U(SIMM16.u16)

name: S_CMPK_GE_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is greater than or equal to the zero extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u >= 32'U(SIMM16.u16)

name: S_CMPK_LT_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is less than the zero extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u < 32'U(SIMM16.u16)

name: S_CMPK_LE_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff scalar input is less than or equal to the zero extension of a literal 16-bit constant.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u <= 32'U(SIMM16.u16)

name: S_ADDK_I32
description: Add a scalar input and the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant, store the result into a scalar register and store the carry-out bit into SCC.

name: S_MULK_I32
description: Multiply a scalar input with the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant and store the result into a scalar register.
pseudocode: D0.i = 32'I(64'I(D0.i) * signext(SIMM16.i16))

name: S_GETREG_B32
description: Read some or all of a hardware register into the LSBs of destination. The SIMM16 argument is encoded as follows: ID = SIMM16[5:0]

name: S_SETREG_B32
description: Write some or all of the LSBs of source argument into a hardware register. The SIMM16 argument is encoded as follows: ID = SIMM16[5:0]

name: S_SETREG_IMM32_B32
description: Write some or all of the LSBs of a 32-bit literal constant into a hardware register; this instruction requires a 32- bit literal constant. The SIMM16 argument is encoded as follows: ID = SIMM16[5:0]

name: S_CALL_B64
description: Store the address of the next instruction to a scalar register and then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction. The byte address of the instruction immediately following this instruction is saved to the destination.
pseudocode:
D0.i64 = PC + 4LL;
  PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
notes:
This implements a short subroutine call where the return address (the next instruction after the S_CALL_B64)
is saved to D. Long calls should consider S_SWAPPC_B64 instead.

This instruction must be 4 bytes.

name: S_WAITCNT_VSCNT
description: Wait for the counts of outstanding vector store events -- vector memory stores and atomics that DO NOT return data -- to be at or below the specified level. This counter is not used in 'all-in-order' mode. Waits for the following condition to hold before continuing:
pseudocode:
vscnt <= S0.u[5:0] + S1.u[5:0].
        // Comparison is 6 bits, no clamping is applied for add overflow

To wait on a literal constant only, write 'null' for the GPR argument.

This opcode may only appear inside a clause if the SGPR operand is set to NULL.

See also S_WAITCNT.

name: S_WAITCNT_VMCNT
description: Wait for the counts of outstanding vector memory events -- everything except for memory stores and atomics- without-return -- to be at or below the specified level. When in 'all-in-order' mode, wait for all vector memory events. Waits for the following condition to hold before continuing:
pseudocode:
vmcnt <= S0.u[5:0] + S1.u[5:0].
      // Comparison is 6 bits, no clamping is applied for add overflow

To wait on a literal constant only, write 'null' for the GPR argument or use S_WAITCNT.

This opcode may only appear inside a clause if the SGPR operand is set to NULL.

See also S_WAITCNT.

name: S_WAITCNT_EXPCNT
description: Wait for the counts of outstanding export events to be at or below the specified level. Waits for the following condition to hold before continuing:
pseudocode:
expcnt <= S0.u[2:0] + S1.u[2:0].
      // Comparison is 3 bits, no clamping is applied for add overflow

To wait on a literal constant only, write 'null' for the GPR argument or use S_WAITCNT.

This opcode may only appear inside a clause if the SGPR operand is set to NULL.

See also S_WAITCNT.

name: S_WAITCNT_LGKMCNT
description: Wait for the counts of outstanding DS (LG), scalar memory (K) and message (M) events to be at or below the specified level. Waits for the following condition to hold before continuing:
pseudocode:
lgkmcnt <= S0.u[5:0] + S1.u[5:0].
      // Comparison is 6 bits, no clamping is applied for add overflow

To wait on a literal constant only, write 'null' for the GPR argument or use S_WAITCNT.

This opcode may only appear inside a clause if the SGPR operand is set to NULL.

See also S_WAITCNT.

name: S_MOV_B32
description: Move scalar input into a scalar register.
pseudocode: D0.b = S0.b

name: S_MOV_B64
description: Move scalar input into a scalar register.

name: S_CMOV_B32
description: Move scalar input into a scalar register iff SCC is nonzero.
pseudocode:
if SCC then
      D0.b = S0.b
  endif

name: S_CMOV_B64
description: Move scalar input into a scalar register iff SCC is nonzero.
pseudocode:
if SCC then
      D0.b64 = S0.b64
  endif

name: S_BREV_B32
description: Reverse the order of bits in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register.

name: S_BREV_B64
description: Reverse the order of bits in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register.

name: S_CTZ_I32_B32
description: Count the number of trailing "0" bits before the first "1" in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits in the input.
notes:
Functional examples:

S_CTZ_I32_B32(0xaaaaaaaa) => 1
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0x55555555) => 0
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0xffffffff) => 0
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0x00010000) => 16

Compare with V_CTZ_I32_B32, which performs the equivalent operation in the vector ALU.

name: S_CTZ_I32_B64
description: Count the number of trailing "0" bits before the first "1" in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits in the input.

name: S_CLZ_I32_U32
description: Count the number of leading "0" bits before the first "1" in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits.
notes:
Functional examples:

S_CLZ_I32_U32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0x0000cccc) => 16
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0xffff3333) => 0
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0x7fffffff) => 1
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0x80000000) => 0
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0xffffffff) => 0

Compare with V_CLZ_I32_U32, which performs the equivalent operation in the vector ALU.

name: S_CLZ_I32_U64
description: Count the number of leading "0" bits before the first "1" in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits.

name: S_CLS_I32
description: Count the number of leading bits that are the same as the sign bit of a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register. Store -1 if all input bits are the same.
notes:
Functional examples:

S_CLS_I32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
S_CLS_I32(0x0000cccc) => 16
S_CLS_I32(0xffff3333) => 16
S_CLS_I32(0x7fffffff) => 1
S_CLS_I32(0x80000000) => 1
S_CLS_I32(0xffffffff) => 0xffffffff

Compare with V_CLS_I32, which performs the equivalent operation in the vector ALU.

name: S_CLS_I32_I64
description: Count the number of leading bits that are the same as the sign bit of a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register. Store -1 if all input bits are the same.

name: S_SEXT_I32_I8
description: Sign extend a signed 8 bit scalar input to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar register.
pseudocode: D0.i = 32'I(signext(S0.i8))

name: S_SEXT_I32_I16
description: Sign extend a signed 16 bit scalar input to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar register.
pseudocode: D0.i = 32'I(signext(S0.i16))

name: S_BITSET0_B32
description: Given a bit offset in a scalar input, set the indicated bit in the destination scalar register to 0.

name: S_BITSET0_B64
description: Given a bit offset in a scalar input, set the indicated bit in the destination scalar register to 0.

name: S_BITSET1_B32
description: Given a bit offset in a scalar input, set the indicated bit in the destination scalar register to 1.

name: S_BITSET1_B64
description: Given a bit offset in a scalar input, set the indicated bit in the destination scalar register to 1.

name: S_BITREPLICATE_B64_B32
description: Substitute each bit of a 32 bit scalar input with two instances of itself and store the result into a 64 bit scalar register.
notes:
This opcode can be used to convert a quad mask into a pixel mask; given quad mask in s0, the following
sequence produces a pixel mask in s2:

s_bitreplicate_b64 s2, s0
s_bitreplicate_b64 s2, s2

To perform the inverse operation see S_QUADMASK_B64.

name: S_ABS_I32
description: Compute the absolute value of a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
notes:
Functional examples:

S_ABS_I32(0x00000001) => 0x00000001
S_ABS_I32(0x7fffffff) => 0x7fffffff
S_ABS_I32(0x80000000) => 0x80000000        // Note this is negative!
S_ABS_I32(0x80000001) => 0x7fffffff
S_ABS_I32(0x80000002) => 0x7ffffffe
S_ABS_I32(0xffffffff) => 0x00000001

name: S_BCNT0_I32_B32
description: Count the number of "0" bits in a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
notes:
Functional examples:

S_BCNT0_I32_B32(0x00000000) => 32
S_BCNT0_I32_B32(0xcccccccc) => 16
S_BCNT0_I32_B32(0xffffffff) => 0

name: S_BCNT0_I32_B64
description: Count the number of "0" bits in a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_BCNT1_I32_B32
description: Count the number of "1" bits in a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
notes:
Functional examples:

S_BCNT1_I32_B32(0x00000000) => 0
S_BCNT1_I32_B32(0xcccccccc) => 16
S_BCNT1_I32_B32(0xffffffff) => 32

name: S_BCNT1_I32_B64
description: Count the number of "1" bits in a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_QUADMASK_B32
description: Reduce a pixel mask from the scalar input into a quad mask, store the result in a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
notes: To perform the inverse operation see S_BITREPLICATE_B64_B32.

name: S_QUADMASK_B64
description: Reduce a pixel mask from the scalar input into a quad mask, store the result in a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
notes: To perform the inverse operation see S_BITREPLICATE_B64_B32.

name: S_WQM_B32
description: Given an active pixel mask in a scalar input, calculate whole quad mode mask for that input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero. In whole quad mode, if any pixel in a quad is active then all pixels of the quad are marked active.

name: S_WQM_B64
description: Given an active pixel mask in a scalar input, calculate whole quad mode mask for that input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero. In whole quad mode, if any pixel in a quad is active then all pixels of the quad are marked active.

name: S_NOT_B32
description: Calculate bitwise negation on a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
pseudocode:
D0.u = ~S0.u;
  SCC = D0.u != 0U

name: S_NOT_B64
description: Calculate bitwise negation on a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.

name: S_AND_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_AND_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_OR_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise OR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_OR_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise OR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_XOR_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise XOR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_XOR_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise XOR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_NAND_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Bitwise NAND with EXEC mask. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_NAND_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Bitwise NAND with EXEC mask. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_NOR_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Bitwise NOR with EXEC mask. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_NOR_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Bitwise NOR with EXEC mask. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_XNOR_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Bitwise XNOR with EXEC mask. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_XNOR_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Bitwise XNOR with EXEC mask. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_AND_NOT0_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_AND_NOT0_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_OR_NOT0_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise OR on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_OR_NOT0_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise OR on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_AND_NOT1_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_AND_NOT1_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_OR_NOT1_SAVEEXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise OR on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_OR_NOT1_SAVEEXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise OR on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination register. The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

name: S_AND_NOT0_WREXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask and also into the scalar destination register, and set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero. Unlike the SAVEEXEC series of opcodes, the value written to destination SGPRs is the result of the bitwise-op result. EXEC and the destination SGPRs have the same value at the end of this instruction. This instruction is intended to help accelerate waterfalling.

name: S_AND_NOT0_WREXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask and also into the scalar destination register, and set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero. Unlike the SAVEEXEC series of opcodes, the value written to destination SGPRs is the result of the bitwise-op result. EXEC and the destination SGPRs have the same value at the end of this instruction. This instruction is intended to help accelerate waterfalling.

name: S_AND_NOT1_WREXEC_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask and also into the scalar destination register, and set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero. Unlike the SAVEEXEC series of opcodes, the value written to destination SGPRs is the result of the bitwise-op result. EXEC and the destination SGPRs have the same value at the end of this instruction. This instruction is intended to help accelerate waterfalling.
notes: See S_AND_NOT1_WREXEC_B64 for example code.

name: S_AND_NOT1_WREXEC_B64
description: Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask and also into the scalar destination register, and set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero. Unlike the SAVEEXEC series of opcodes, the value written to destination SGPRs is the result of the bitwise-op result. EXEC and the destination SGPRs have the same value at the end of this instruction. This instruction is intended to help accelerate waterfalling.

name: S_MOVRELS_B32
description: Move data from a relatively-indexed scalar register into another scalar register.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move s5 <= s17:

s_mov_b32 m0, 10
s_movrels_b32 s5, s7

name: S_MOVRELS_B64
description: Move data from a relatively-indexed scalar register into another scalar register. The index in M0.u and the operand address in SRC0.u must be even for this operation.

name: S_MOVRELD_B32
description: Move data from a scalar input into a relatively-indexed scalar register.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move s15 <= s7:

s_mov_b32 m0, 10
s_movreld_b32 s5, s7

name: S_MOVRELD_B64
description: Move data from a scalar input into a relatively-indexed scalar register. The index in M0.u and the operand address in DST.u must be even for this operation.

name: S_MOVRELSD_2_B32
description: Move data from a relatively-indexed scalar register into another relatively-indexed scalar register, using different offsets for each index.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move s25 <= s17:

s_mov_b32 m0, ((20 << 16) | 10)
s_movrelsd_2_b32 s5, s7

name: S_GETPC_B64
description: Store the address of the next instruction to a scalar register. The byte address of the instruction immediately following this instruction is saved to the destination.
pseudocode: D0.i64 = PC + 4LL
notes: This instruction must be 4 bytes.

name: S_SETPC_B64
description: Jump to an address specified in a scalar register. The argument is a byte address of the instruction to jump to.
pseudocode: PC = S0.i64

name: S_SWAPPC_B64
description: Store the address of the next instruction to a scalar register and then jump to an address specified in the scalar input. The argument is a byte address of the instruction to jump to. The byte address of the instruction immediately following this instruction is saved to the destination.
notes: This instruction must be 4 bytes.

name: S_RFE_B64
description: Return from the exception handler. Clear the wave's PRIV bit and then jump to an address specified by the scalar input. The argument is a byte address of the instruction to jump to; this address is likely derived from the state passed into the trap handler. This instruction may only be used within a trap handler.
pseudocode:
WAVE_STATUS.PRIV = 1'0U;
  PC = S0.i64

name: S_SENDMSG_RTN_B32
description: Send a message to upstream control hardware. SSRC[7:0] contains the message type encoded in the instruction directly (this instruction does not read an SGPR). The message is expected to return a response from the upstream control hardware and the result is written to SDST. Use s_waitcnt lgkmcnt(…) to wait for the response on the dependent instruction. S_SENDMSG_RTN* instructions return data in-order among themselves but out-of-order with other instructions that manipulate lgkmcnt (including S_SENDMSG and S_SENDMSGHALT). If the message returns a 64 bit value then only the lower 32 bits are written to SDST. If SDST is VCC then VCCZ is undefined.

name: S_SENDMSG_RTN_B64
description: Send a message to upstream control hardware. SSRC[7:0] contains the message type encoded in the instruction directly (this instruction does not read an SGPR). The message is expected to return a response from the upstream control hardware and the result is written to SDST. Use s_waitcnt lgkmcnt(…) to wait for the response on the dependent instruction. S_SENDMSG_RTN* instructions return data in-order among themselves but out-of-order with other instructions that manipulate lgkmcnt (including S_SENDMSG and S_SENDMSGHALT). If the message returns a 32 bit value then this instruction fills the upper bits of SDST with zero. If SDST is VCC then VCCZ is undefined.

name: S_CMP_EQ_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is equal to the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.i == S1.i
notes: Note that S_CMP_EQ_I32 and S_CMP_EQ_U32 are identical opcodes, but both are provided for symmetry.

name: S_CMP_LG_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or greater than the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.i <> S1.i
notes: Note that S_CMP_LG_I32 and S_CMP_LG_U32 are identical opcodes, but both are provided for symmetry.

name: S_CMP_GT_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.i > S1.i

name: S_CMP_GE_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than or equal to the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.i >= S1.i

name: S_CMP_LT_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.i < S1.i

name: S_CMP_LE_I32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or equal to the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.i <= S1.i

name: S_CMP_EQ_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is equal to the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u == S1.u
notes: Note that S_CMP_EQ_I32 and S_CMP_EQ_U32 are identical opcodes, but both are provided for symmetry.

name: S_CMP_LG_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or greater than the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u <> S1.u
notes: Note that S_CMP_LG_I32 and S_CMP_LG_U32 are identical opcodes, but both are provided for symmetry.

name: S_CMP_GT_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u > S1.u

name: S_CMP_GE_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than or equal to the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u >= S1.u

name: S_CMP_LT_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u < S1.u

name: S_CMP_LE_U32
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or equal to the second scalar input.
pseudocode: SCC = S0.u <= S1.u

name: S_BITCMP0_B32
description: Extract a bit from the first scalar input based on an index in the second scalar input, and set SCC to 1 iff the extracted bit is equal to 0.

name: S_BITCMP1_B32
description: Extract a bit from the first scalar input based on an index in the second scalar input, and set SCC to 1 iff the extracted bit is equal to 1.

name: S_BITCMP0_B64
description: Extract a bit from the first scalar input based on an index in the second scalar input, and set SCC to 1 iff the extracted bit is equal to 0.

name: S_BITCMP1_B64
description: Extract a bit from the first scalar input based on an index in the second scalar input, and set SCC to 1 iff the extracted bit is equal to 1.

name: S_CMP_EQ_U64
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is equal to the second scalar input.

name: S_CMP_LG_U64
description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or greater than the second scalar input.

name: S_NOP
description: Do nothing. Delay issue of next instruction by a small, fixed amount. Insert 0..15 wait states based on SIMM16[3:0]. 0x0 means the next instruction can issue on the next clock, 0xf means the next instruction can issue 16 clocks later.
pseudocode:
for i in 0U : SIMM16.u16[3 : 0].u do
        nop()
  endfor
notes:
Examples:

s_nop 0           // Wait 1 cycle.
s_nop 0xf         // Wait 16 cycles.

name: S_SETKILL
description: Kill this wave if the least significant bit of the immediate constant is 1. Used primarily for debugging kill wave host command behavior.

name: S_SETHALT
description: Set or clear the HALT or FATAL_HALT status bits. The particular status bit is chosen by halt type control as indicated in SIMM16[2]; 0 = HALT bit select; 1 = FATAL_HALT bit select. When halt type control is set to 0 (HALT bit select): Set HALT bit to value of SIMM16[0]; 1 = halt, 0 = clear HALT bit. The halt flag is ignored while PRIV == 1 (inside trap handlers) but the shader halts after the handler returns if HALT is still set at that time. When halt type control is set to 1 (FATAL HALT bit select): Set FATAL_HALT bit to value of SIMM16[0]; 1 = fatal_halt, 0 = clear FATAL_HALT bit. Setting the fatal_halt flag halts the shader in or outside of the trap handlers.

name: S_SLEEP
description: Cause a wave to sleep for up to ~8000 clocks. The wave sleeps for (64*(SIMM16[6:0]-1) .. 64*SIMM16[6:0]) clocks. The exact amount of delay is approximate. Compare with S_NOP. When SIMM16[6:0] is zero then no sleep occurs.
notes:
Examples:

s_sleep 0         // Wait for 0 clocks.
s_sleep 1         // Wait for 1-64 clocks.
s_sleep 2         // Wait for 65-128 clocks.

name: S_SET_INST_PREFETCH_DISTANCE
description: Change instruction prefetch mode. This controls how many cachelines ahead of the current PC the shader will try to prefetch. SIMM16[1:0] specifies the prefetch mode to switch to. Prefetch modes are: PREFETCH_SAFE (0x0)
pseudocode:
Reserved, do not use.

PREFETCH_1_LINE (0x1)
   Prefetch 1 cache line ahead of PC; keep 2 lines behind PC.

PREFETCH_2_LINES (0x2)
   Prefetch 2 cache lines ahead of PC; keep 1 line behind PC.

PREFETCH_3_LINES (0x3)
   Prefetch 3 cache lines ahead of PC; keep 0 lines behind PC.

SIMM16[15:2] must be set to zero.

name: S_CLAUSE
description: Mark the beginning of a clause. The next instruction determines the clause type, which may be one of the following types.
pseudocode:
• Image Load (non-sample instructions )
  • Image Sample
  • Image Store
  • Image Atomic

  • Buffer/Global/Scratch Load
  • Buffer/Global/Scratch Store
  • Buffer/Global/Scratch Atomic
  • Flat Load
  • Flat Store
  • Flat Atomic
  • LDS (loads, stores, atomics may be in same clause)
  • Scalar Memory
  • Vector ALU

Once the clause type is determined, any instruction encountered within the clause that is not of the same type
(and not an internal instruction described below) is illegal and may lead to undefined behaviour. Attempting to
issue S_CLAUSE while inside a clause is also illegal.

Instructions that are processed internally do not interrupt the clause. The following instructions are internal:

  • S_NOP,
  • S_WAITCNT and its variants, unless they read an SGPR,
  • S_SLEEP,
  • S_DELAY_ALU.

Halting or killing a wave breaks the clause. VALU exceptions and other traps that cause the shader to enter its
trap handler breaks the clause. The single-step debug mode breaks the clause.

The clause length must be between 2 and 63 instructions, inclusive. Clause breaks may be from 1 to 15, or may
be disabled entirely. Clause length and breaks are encoded in the SIMM16 argument as follows:

LENGTH = SIMM16[5:0]
   This field is set to the logical number of instructions in the clause, minus 1 (e.g. if a clause has 4
   instructions, program this field to 3). The minimum number of instructions required for a clause is 2 and
   the maximum number of instructions is 63, therefore this field must be programmed in the range [1, 62]
   inclusive.

BREAK_SPAN = SIMM16[11:8]
   This field is set to the number of instructions to issue before each clause break. If set to zero then there are
   no clause breaks. If set to nonzero value then the maximum number of instructions between clause breaks
   is 15.

The following instruction types cannot appear in a clause:

  • SALU
  • Export
  • Branch
  • Message
  • LDSDIR
  • VINTERP
  • GDS

To schedule an S_WAITCNT or S_DELAY_ALU instruction for the first instruction in the clause, the
waitcnt/delay instruction must appear before the S_CLAUSE instruction so that S_CLAUSE can accurately

determine the clause type.

S_DELAY_ALU must not appear inside a clause. The features are orthogonal; ALU clauses should be structured
to avoid any stalling.

name: S_DELAY_ALU
description: Insert delay between dependent SALU/VALU instructions. The SIMM16 argument is encoded as: INSTID0 = SIMM16[3:0]
pseudocode:
Hazard to delay for with the next VALU instruction.

INSTSKIP = SIMM16[6:4]
   Identify the VALU instruction that the second delay condition applies to.

INSTID1 = SIMM16[10:7]
   Hazard to delay for with the VALU instruction identified by INSTSKIP.

Legal values for the InstID0 and InstID1 fields are:

INSTID_NO_DEP (0x0)
   No dependency on any prior instruction.

INSTID_VALU_DEP_1 (0x1)
   Dependent on previous VALU instruction, 1 instruction back.

INSTID_VALU_DEP_2 (0x2)
   Dependent on previous VALU instruction, 2 instructions back.

INSTID_VALU_DEP_3 (0x3)
   Dependent on previous VALU instruction, 3 instructions back.

INSTID_VALU_DEP_4 (0x4)
   Dependent on previous VALU instruction, 4 instructions back.

INSTID_TRANS32_DEP_1 (0x5)
   Dependent on previous TRANS32 instruction, 1 instruction back.

INSTID_TRANS32_DEP_2 (0x6)
   Dependent on previous TRANS32 instruction, 2 instructions back.

INSTID_TRANS32_DEP_3 (0x7)
   Dependent on previous TRANS32 instruction, 3 instructions back.

INSTID_FMA_ACCUM_CYCLE_1 (0x8)
   Single cycle penalty for FMA accumulation (reserved).

INSTID_SALU_CYCLE_1 (0x9)
   1 cycle penalty for a prior SALU instruction.

INSTID_SALU_CYCLE_2 (0xa)
   2 cycle penalty for a prior SALU instruction (reserved).

INSTID_SALU_CYCLE_3 (0xb)
   3 cycle penalty for a prior SALU instruction (reserved).

Legal values for the InstSkip field are:

INSTSKIP_SAME (0x0)
   Apply second dependency to same instruction (2 dependencies on one instruction).

INSTSKIP_NEXT (0x1)
   Apply second dependency to next instruction (no skip).

INSTSKIP_SKIP_1 (0x2)
   Skip 1 instruction then apply dependency.

INSTSKIP_SKIP_2 (0x3)
   Skip 2 instructions then apply dependency.

INSTSKIP_SKIP_3 (0x4)
   Skip 3 instructions then apply dependency.

INSTSKIP_SKIP_4 (0x5)
   Skip 4 instructions then apply dependency.

This instruction describes dependencies for two instructions, directing the hardware to insert delay if the
dependent instruction was issued too recently to forward data to the second.

S_DELAY_ALU instructions record the required delay with respect to a previous VALU instruction and indicate
data dependencies that benefit from having extra idle cycles inserted between them. These instructions are
optional: without them the program still functions correctly but performance may suffer when multiple waves
are in flight; IB may issue dependent instructions that stall in the ALU, preventing those cycles from being
utilized by other wavefronts.

If enough independent instructions are between dependent ones then no delay is necessary and this
instruction may be omitted. For wave64 the compiler may not know the status of the EXEC mask and hence
does not know if instructions require 1 or 2 passes to issue. S_DELAY_ALU encodes the type of dependency so
that hardware may apply the correct delay depending on the number of active passes.

S_DELAY_ALU may execute in zero cycles.

To reduce instruction stream overhead the S_DELAY_ALU instructions packs two delay values into one
instruction, with a "skip" indicator so the two delayed instructions don't need to be back-to-back.

S_DELAY_ALU is illegal inside of a clause created by S_CLAUSE.

Example:

  v_mov_b32 v3, v0
  v_lshlrev_b32     v30, 1, v31
  v_lshlrev_b32     v24, 1, v25
  s_delay_alu instid0(INSTID_VALU_DEP_3) | instskip(INSTSKIP_SKIP_1) | instid1(INSTID_VALU_DEP_1)
      // 1 cycle delay here
  v_add_f32   v0, v1, v3
  v_sub_f32   v11, v9, v9
      // 2 cycles delay here
  v_mul_f32   v10, v13, v11

name: S_WAITCNT
description: Wait for the counts of outstanding lds, vector-memory and export/vmem-write-data to be at or below the specified levels. The SIMM16 argument is encoded as: EXP = SIMM16[2:0]
pseudocode:
Export wait count. 0x7 means do not wait on EXPCNT.

LGKM = SIMM16[9:4]
   LGKM wait count. 0x3f means do not wait on LGKMCNT.

VM = SIMM16[15:10]
   VM wait count. 0x3f means do not wait on VMCNT.

Waits for all of the following conditions to hold before continuing:

      expcnt <= WaitEXPCNT
      lgkmcnt <= WaitLGKMCNT
      vmcnt <= WaitVMCNT

VMCNT only counts vector memory loads, image sample instructions, and vector memory atomics that return
data. Contrast with the VSCNT counter.

See also S_WAITCNT_VSCNT.

name: S_WAIT_IDLE
description: Wait for all activity in the wave to be complete (all dependency and memory counters at zero).

name: S_WAIT_EVENT
description: Wait for an event to occur or a condition to be satisfied before continuing. The SIMM16 argument specifies which event(s) to wait on. DONT_WAIT_EXPORT_READY = SIMM16[0]
pseudocode:
If this value is ZERO then sleep until the export_ready bit is 1. If the export_ready bit is already 1, no sleep
   occurs. Effect is the same as the export_ready check performed before issuing an export instruction.

No wait occurs if this value is ONE.

This wait can be broken or preempted by KILL, context-save, host trap, single-step or trap after instruction
events. IB waits for the event to occur before processing internal exceptions which can delay entry to the trap
handler for a significant amount of time.

name: S_TRAP
description: Enter the trap handler. This instruction may be generated internally as well in response to a host trap (HT = 1) or an exception. TrapID 0 is reserved for hardware use and should not be used in a shader-generated trap.

name: S_ROUND_MODE
description: Set floating point round mode using an immediate constant. Avoids wait state penalty that would be imposed by S_SETREG.

name: S_DENORM_MODE
description: Set floating point denormal mode using an immediate constant. Avoids wait state penalty that would be imposed by S_SETREG.

name: S_CODE_END
description: Generate an illegal instruction interrupt. This instruction is used to mark the end of a shader buffer for debug tools. This instruction should not appear in typical shader code. It is used to pad the end of a shader program to make it easier for analysis programs to locate the end of a shader program buffer. Use of this opcode in an embedded shader block may cause analysis tools to fail. To unambiguously mark the end of a shader buffer, this instruction must be specified five times in a row (total of 20 bytes) and analysis tools must ensure the opcode occurs at least five times to be certain they are at the end of the buffer. This is because the bit pattern generated by this opcode could incidentally appear in a valid instruction's second dword, literal constant or as part of a multi-DWORD image instruction. In short: do not embed this opcode in the middle of a valid shader program. DO use this opcode 5 times at the end of a shader program to clearly mark the end of the program. Example:
pseudocode:
...
        s_endpgm       // last real instruction in shader buffer
        s_code_end        // 1
        s_code_end        // 2
        s_code_end        // 3
        s_code_end        // 4
        s_code_end        // done!

name: S_BRANCH
description: Jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL;
  // short jump.
notes:
For a long jump or an indirect jump use S_SETPC_B64.

Examples:

s_branch label      // Set SIMM16 = +4 = 0x0004
s_nop 0      // 4 bytes
label:
s_nop 0      // 4 bytes
s_branch label      // Set SIMM16 = -8 = 0xfff8

name: S_CBRANCH_SCC0
description: If SCC is 0 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if SCC == 1'0U then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_SCC1
description: If SCC is 1 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if SCC == 1'1U then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_VCCZ
description: If VCCZ is 1 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if VCCZ.u1 == 1'1U then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_VCCNZ
description: If VCCZ is 0 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if VCCZ.u1 == 1'0U then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else

      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_EXECZ
description: If EXECZ is 1 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if EXECZ.u1 == 1'1U then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_EXECNZ
description: If EXECZ is 0 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if EXECZ.u1 == 1'0U then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_CDBGSYS
description: If the system debug flag is set then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if WAVE_STATUS.COND_DBG_SYS.u != 0U then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_CDBGUSER
description: If the user debug flag is set then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if WAVE_STATUS.COND_DBG_USER.u != 0U then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_CDBGSYS_OR_USER
description: If either the system debug flag or the user debug flag is set then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if (WAVE_STATUS.COND_DBG_SYS || WAVE_STATUS.COND_DBG_USER) then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_CBRANCH_CDBGSYS_AND_USER
description: If both the system debug flag and the user debug flag are set then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC. The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
pseudocode:
if (WAVE_STATUS.COND_DBG_SYS && WAVE_STATUS.COND_DBG_USER) then
      PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
  else
      PC = PC + 4LL
  endif

name: S_ENDPGM
description: End of program; terminate wavefront. The hardware implicitly executes S_WAITCNT 0 and S_WAITCNT_VSCNT 0 before executing this instruction. See S_ENDPGM_SAVED for the context-switch version of this instruction and S_ENDPGM_ORDERED_PS_DONE for the POPS critical region version of this instruction.

name: S_ENDPGM_SAVED
description: End of program; signal that a wave has been saved by the context-switch trap handler and terminate wavefront. The hardware implicitly executes S_WAITCNT 0 and S_WAITCNT_VSCNT 0 before executing this instruction. See S_ENDPGM for additional variants.

name: S_ENDPGM_ORDERED_PS_DONE
description: End of program; signal that a wave has exited its POPS critical section and terminate wavefront. The hardware implicitly executes S_WAITCNT 0 and S_WAITCNT_VSCNT 0 before executing this instruction. This instruction is an optimization that combines S_SENDMSG(MSG_ORDERED_PS_DONE) and S_ENDPGM; there may be cases where the message needs to be sent separately, in which case the shader can be terminated with a normal S_ENDPGM instruction. See S_ENDPGM for additional variants.

name: S_WAKEUP
description: Allow a wave to 'ping' all the other waves in its threadgroup to force them to wake up early from an S_SLEEP instruction. The ping is ignored if the waves are not sleeping. This allows for efficient polling on a memory location. The waves which are polling can sit in a long S_SLEEP between memory reads, but the wave which writes the value can tell them all to wake up early now that the data is available. This method is also safe from races because if any wave misses the ping, everything is expected to work fine (waves which missed it just complete their S_SLEEP). If the wave executing S_WAKEUP is in a threadgroup (in_wg set), then it wakes up all waves associated with the same threadgroup ID. Otherwise, S_WAKEUP is treated as an S_NOP.

name: S_SETPRIO
description: Change wave user priority. User settable wave priority is set to SIMM16[1:0]. 0 is the lowest priority and 3 is the highest. The overall wave priority is:

name: S_SENDMSG
description: Send a message to upstream control hardware. SIMM16[7:0] contains the message type.

name: S_SENDMSGHALT
description: Send a message to upstream control hardware and then HALT the wavefront; see S_SENDMSG for details.

name: S_INCPERFLEVEL
description: Increment performance counter specified in SIMM16[3:0] by 1.

name: S_DECPERFLEVEL
description: Decrement performance counter specified in SIMM16[3:0] by 1.

name: S_ICACHE_INV
description: Invalidate entire first level instruction cache.

name: S_BARRIER
description: Synchronize waves within a threadgroup. If not all waves of the threadgroup have been created yet, waits for entire group before proceeding. If some waves in the threadgroup have already terminated, this waits on only the surviving waves. Barriers are legal inside trap handlers. Barrier instructions do not wait for any counters to go to zero before issuing. If the barrier is being used to protect an outstanding memory operation use the appropriate S_WAITCNT instruction before the barrier.

name: S_LOAD_B32
description: Load 32 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register.
pseudocode: SDATA[31 : 0] = MEM[ADDR + 0U].b
notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).

If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

name: S_LOAD_B64
description: Load 64 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register.
pseudocode:
SDATA[31 : 0] = MEM[ADDR + 0U].b;
  SDATA[63 : 32] = MEM[ADDR + 4U].b
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_LOAD_B128
description: Load 128 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register.
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_LOAD_B256
description: Load 256 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register.
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_LOAD_B512
description: Load 512 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register.
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_BUFFER_LOAD_B32
description: Load 32 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register using a buffer resource descriptor.
pseudocode: SDATA[31 : 0] = MEM[ADDR + 0U].b
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_BUFFER_LOAD_B64
description: Load 64 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register using a buffer resource descriptor.
pseudocode:
SDATA[31 : 0] = MEM[ADDR + 0U].b;
  SDATA[63 : 32] = MEM[ADDR + 4U].b
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_BUFFER_LOAD_B128
description: Load 128 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register using a buffer resource descriptor.
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_BUFFER_LOAD_B256
description: Load 256 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register using a buffer resource descriptor.
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_BUFFER_LOAD_B512
description: Load 512 bits of data from the scalar data cache into a scalar register using a buffer resource descriptor.
notes: See S_LOAD_B32 for details on the offset input.

name: S_GL1_INV
description: Invalidate the GL1 cache only.

name: S_DCACHE_INV
description: Invalidate the scalar data L0 cache.

name: V_CNDMASK_B32
description: Copy data from one of two inputs based on the vector condition code and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u = VCC.u64[laneId] ? S1.u : S0.u
notes:
In VOP3 the VCC source may be a scalar GPR specified in S2.

Floating-point modifiers are valid for this instruction if S0 and S1 are 32-bit floating point values. This
instruction is suitable for negating or taking the absolute value of a floating-point value.

name: V_DOT2ACC_F32_F16
description: Dot product of packed FP16 values, accumulate with destination.

name: V_ADD_F32
description: Add two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S0.f + S1.f
notes: 0.5ULP precision, denormals are supported.

name: V_SUB_F32
description: Subtract the second floating point input from the first input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S0.f - S1.f
notes: 0.5ULP precision, denormals are supported.

name: V_SUBREV_F32
description: Subtract the first floating point input from the second input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S1.f - S0.f
notes: 0.5ULP precision, denormals are supported.

name: V_FMAC_DX9_ZERO_F32
description: Multiply two single-precision values and accumulate the result with the destination. Follows DX9 rules where 0.0 times anything produces 0.0 (this is not IEEE compliant).
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f) == 0.0) || (64'F(S1.f) == 0.0)) then
        // DX9 rules, 0.0 * x = 0.0
        D0.f = S2.f
  else
        D0.f = fma(S0.f, S1.f, D0.f)
  endif

name: V_MUL_DX9_ZERO_F32
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and store the result in a vector register. Follows DX9 rules where 0.0 times anything produces 0.0 (this differs from other APIs when the other input is infinity or NaN).

name: V_MUL_F32
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S0.f * S1.f
notes: 0.5ULP precision, denormals are supported.

name: V_MUL_I32_I24
description: Multiply two signed 24 bit integer inputs and store the result as a signed 32 bit integer into a vector register.
notes:
This opcode is expected to be as efficient as basic single-precision opcodes since it utilizes the single-precision
floating point multiplier. See also V_MUL_HI_I32_I24.

name: V_MUL_HI_I32_I24
description: Multiply two signed 24 bit integer inputs and store the high 32 bits of the result as a signed 32 bit integer into a vector register.
notes: See also V_MUL_I32_I24.

name: V_MUL_U32_U24
description: Multiply two unsigned 24 bit integer inputs and store the result as a unsigned 32 bit integer into a vector register.
notes:
This opcode is expected to be as efficient as basic single-precision opcodes since it utilizes the single-precision
floating point multiplier. See also V_MUL_HI_U32_U24.

name: V_MUL_HI_U32_U24
description: Multiply two unsigned 24 bit integer inputs and store the high 32 bits of the result as a unsigned 32 bit integer into a vector register.
notes: See also V_MUL_U32_U24.

name: V_MIN_F32
description: Select the minimum of two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
LT_NEG_ZERO = lambda(a, b) (
        ((a < b) || ((64'F(abs(a)) == 0.0) && (64'F(abs(b)) == 0.0) && sign(a) && !sign(b))));
  // Version of comparison where -0.0 < +0.0, differs from IEEE
  if WAVE_MODE.IEEE then
        if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = 32'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f)))
        elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = 32'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f)))
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = S0.f
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = S1.f
        elsif LT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f, S1.f) then
            // NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f = S0.f
        else
            D0.f = S1.f
        endif
  else
        if isNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = S0.f
        elsif isNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = S1.f
        elsif LT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f, S1.f) then
            // NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f = S0.f
        else
            D0.f = S1.f
        endif
  endif;
  // Inequalities in the above pseudocode behave differently from IEEE

  // when both inputs are +-0.
notes:
IEEE compliant. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags, saturation.

Denorm flushing for this operation is effectively controlled by the input denorm mode control: If input
denorm mode is disabling denorm, the internal result of a min/max operation cannot be a denorm value, so
output denorm mode is irrelevant. If input denorm mode is enabling denorm, the internal min/max result can
be a denorm and this operation outputs as a denorm regardless of output denorm mode.

name: V_MAX_F32
description: Select the maximum of two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
GT_NEG_ZERO = lambda(a, b) (
        ((a > b) || ((64'F(abs(a)) == 0.0) && (64'F(abs(b)) == 0.0) && !sign(a) && sign(b))));
  // Version of comparison where +0.0 > -0.0, differs from IEEE
  if WAVE_MODE.IEEE then
        if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = 32'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f)))
        elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = 32'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f)))
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = S0.f
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = S1.f
        elsif GT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f, S1.f) then
            // NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f = S0.f
        else
            D0.f = S1.f
        endif
  else
        if isNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = S0.f
        elsif isNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = S1.f
        elsif GT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f, S1.f) then
            // NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f = S0.f
        else
            D0.f = S1.f
        endif
  endif;
  // Inequalities in the above pseudocode behave differently from IEEE
  // when both inputs are +-0.
notes:
IEEE compliant. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags, saturation.

Denorm flushing for this operation is effectively controlled by the input denorm mode control: If input
denorm mode is disabling denorm, the internal result of a min/max operation cannot be a denorm value, so
output denorm mode is irrelevant. If input denorm mode is enabling denorm, the internal min/max result can
be a denorm and this operation outputs as a denorm regardless of output denorm mode.

name: V_MIN_I32
description: Select the minimum of two signed integers and store the selected value into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i = S0.i < S1.i ? S0.i : S1.i

name: V_MAX_I32
description: Select the maximum of two signed integers and store the selected value into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i = S0.i >= S1.i ? S0.i : S1.i

name: V_MIN_U32
description: Select the minimum of two unsigned integers and store the selected value into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u = S0.u < S1.u ? S0.u : S1.u

name: V_MAX_U32
description: Select the maximum of two unsigned integers and store the selected value into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u = S0.u >= S1.u ? S0.u : S1.u

name: V_LSHLREV_B32
description: Given a shift count in the first vector input, calculate the logical shift left of the second vector input and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_LSHRREV_B32
description: Given a shift count in the first vector input, calculate the logical shift right of the second vector input and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_ASHRREV_I32
description: Given a shift count in the first vector input, calculate the arithmetic shift right (preserving sign bit) of the second vector input and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_AND_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND on two vector inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_OR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise OR on two vector inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_XOR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise XOR on two vector inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_XNOR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise XNOR on two vector inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_ADD_CO_CI_U32
description: Add two unsigned inputs and a bit from a carry-in mask, store the result into a vector register and store the carry-out mask into a scalar register.
notes:
In VOP3 the VCC destination may be an arbitrary SGPR-pair, and the VCC source comes from the SGPR-pair at
S2.u.

Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_SUB_CO_CI_U32
description: Subtract the second unsigned input from the first input, subtract a bit from the carry-in mask, store the result into a vector register and store the carry-out mask to a scalar register.
notes:
In VOP3 the VCC destination may be an arbitrary SGPR-pair, and the VCC source comes from the SGPR-pair at
S2.u.

Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_SUBREV_CO_CI_U32
description: Subtract the first unsigned input from the second input, subtract a bit from the carry-in mask, store the result into a vector register and store the carry-out mask to a scalar register.
notes:
In VOP3 the VCC destination may be an arbitrary SGPR-pair, and the VCC source comes from the SGPR-pair at
S2.u.

Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_ADD_NC_U32
description: Add two unsigned inputs and store the result into a vector register. No carry-in or carry-out support.
pseudocode: D0.u = S0.u + S1.u
notes: Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_SUB_NC_U32
description: Subtract the second unsigned input from the first input and store the result into a vector register. No carry-in or carry-out support.
pseudocode: D0.u = S0.u - S1.u
notes: Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_SUBREV_NC_U32
description: Subtract the first unsigned input from the second input and store the result into a vector register. No carry-in or carry-out support.
pseudocode: D0.u = S1.u - S0.u
notes: Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_FMAC_F32
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and accumulate the result into the destination register using fused multiply- add.
pseudocode: D0.f = fma(S0.f, S1.f, D0.f)

name: V_FMAMK_F32
description: Multiply a single-precision float with a literal constant and add a second single-precision float using fused multiply-add.
pseudocode: D0.f = fma(S0.f, SIMM32.f, S1.f)
notes: This opcode cannot use the VOP3 encoding and cannot use input/output modifiers.

name: V_FMAAK_F32
description: Multiply two single-precision floats and add a literal constant using fused multiply-add.
pseudocode: D0.f = fma(S0.f, S1.f, SIMM32.f)
notes: This opcode cannot use the VOP3 encoding and cannot use input/output modifiers.

name: V_CVT_PK_RTZ_F16_F32
description: Convert two single-precision float inputs into a packed FP16 result with round toward zero semantics (ignore the current rounding mode), and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
This opcode is intended for use with 16-bit compressed exports. See V_CVT_F16_F32 for a version that respects
the current rounding mode.

name: V_ADD_F16
description: Add two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_SUB_F16
description: Subtract the second floating point input from the first input and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_SUBREV_F16
description: Subtract the first floating point input from the second input and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_MUL_F16
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_FMAC_F16
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and accumulate the result into the destination register using fused multiply- add.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_FMAMK_F16
description: Multiply a FP16 value with a literal constant and add a second FP16 value using fused multiply-add.
notes:
This opcode cannot use the VOP3 encoding and cannot use input/output modifiers. Supports round mode,
exception flags, saturation.

name: V_FMAAK_F16
description: Multiply two FP16 values and add a literal constant using fused multiply-add.
notes:
This opcode cannot use the VOP3 encoding and cannot use input/output modifiers. Supports round mode,
exception flags, saturation.

name: V_MAX_F16
description: Select the maximum of two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
GT_NEG_ZERO = lambda(a, b) (
        ((a > b) || ((64'F(abs(a)) == 0.0) && (64'F(abs(b)) == 0.0) && !sign(a) && sign(b))));
  // Version of comparison where +0.0 > -0.0, differs from IEEE
  if WAVE_MODE.IEEE then
        if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)))
        elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)))
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        elsif GT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f16, S1.f16) then
            // NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        else
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        endif
  else
        if isNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        elsif isNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        elsif GT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f16, S1.f16) then
            // NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        else
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        endif
  endif;
  // Inequalities in the above pseudocode behave differently from IEEE
  // when both inputs are +-0.
notes:
IEEE compliant. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags, saturation.

Denorm flushing for this operation is effectively controlled by the input denorm mode control: If input
denorm mode is disabling denorm, the internal result of a min/max operation cannot be a denorm value, so
output denorm mode is irrelevant. If input denorm mode is enabling denorm, the internal min/max result can

be a denorm and this operation outputs as a denorm regardless of output denorm mode.

name: V_MIN_F16
description: Select the minimum of two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
LT_NEG_ZERO = lambda(a, b) (
        ((a < b) || ((64'F(abs(a)) == 0.0) && (64'F(abs(b)) == 0.0) && sign(a) && !sign(b))));
  // Version of comparison where -0.0 < +0.0, differs from IEEE
  if WAVE_MODE.IEEE then
        if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)))
        elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)))
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        elsif LT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f16, S1.f16) then
            // NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        else
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        endif
  else
        if isNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        elsif isNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        elsif LT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f16, S1.f16) then
            // NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        else
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        endif
  endif;
  // Inequalities in the above pseudocode behave differently from IEEE
  // when both inputs are +-0.
notes:
IEEE compliant. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags, saturation.

Denorm flushing for this operation is effectively controlled by the input denorm mode control: If input
denorm mode is disabling denorm, the internal result of a min/max operation cannot be a denorm value, so
output denorm mode is irrelevant. If input denorm mode is enabling denorm, the internal min/max result can
be a denorm and this operation outputs as a denorm regardless of output denorm mode.

name: V_LDEXP_F16
description: Multiply the first input, a floating point value, by an integral power of 2 specified in the second input, a signed integer value, and store the floating point result into a vector register. Compare with the ldexp() function in C.

name: V_PK_FMAC_F16
description: Multiply packed FP16 values and accumulate with destination.
notes:
VOP2 version of V_PK_FMA_F16 with third source VGPR address is the destination.

16.7.1. VOP2 using VOP3 or VOP3SD encoding
Instructions in this format may also be encoded as VOP3. VOP3 allows access to the extra control bits (e.g. ABS,
OMOD) at the expense of a larger instruction word. The VOP3 opcode is: VOP2 opcode + 0x100.

name: V_NOP
description: Do nothing.

name: V_MOV_B32
description: Move data from a vector input into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.b = S0.b
notes:
Floating-point modifiers are valid for this instruction if S0.u is a 32-bit floating point value. This instruction is
suitable for negating or taking the absolute value of a floating-point value.

Functional examples:

v_mov_b32 v0, v1    // Move v1 to v0
v_mov_b32 v0, -v1   // Set v1 to the negation of v0
v_mov_b32 v0, abs(v1)   // Set v1 to the absolute value of v0

name: V_READFIRSTLANE_B32
description: Read the scalar value in the lowest active lane of the input vector register and store it into a scalar register.
notes:
Overrides EXEC mask for the VGPR read. Input and output modifiers not supported; this is an untyped
operation.

name: V_CVT_I32_F64
