description: Convert from a double-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, out-of-range floating point values (including infinity) saturate. NAN is converted to 0.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_F64_I32
description: Convert from a signed 32-bit integer input to a double-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0ULP accuracy.

name: V_CVT_F32_I32
description: Convert from a signed 32-bit integer input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy.

name: V_CVT_F32_U32
description: Convert from an unsigned 32-bit integer input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy.

name: V_CVT_U32_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to an unsigned 32-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy, out-of-range floating point values (including infinity) saturate. NAN is converted to 0.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_I32_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy, out-of-range floating point values (including infinity) saturate. NAN is converted to 0.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_F16_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to an FP16 float and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, supports input modifiers and creates FP16 denormals when appropriate. Flush denorms on
output if specified based on DP denorm mode. Output rounding based on DP rounding mode.

name: V_CVT_F32_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0ULP accuracy, FP16 denormal inputs are accepted. Flush denorms on input if specified based on DP denorm
mode.

name: V_CVT_NEAREST_I32_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer using round-to-nearest-integer semantics (ignore the default rounding mode) and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i = f32_to_i32(floor(S0.f + 0.5F))
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_FLOOR_I32_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer using round-down semantics (ignore the default rounding mode) and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i = f32_to_i32(floor(S0.f))
notes: 1ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_OFF_F32_I4
description: Convert from a signed 4-bit integer to a single-precision float using an offset table and store the result into a vector register. Used for interpolation in shader. Lookup table on S0[3:0]: S0 binary Result 1000 -0.5000f 1001 -0.4375f 1010 -0.3750f 1011 -0.3125f 1100 -0.2500f 1101 -0.1875f 1110 -0.1250f 1111 -0.0625f 0000 +0.0000f 0001 +0.0625f 0010 +0.1250f 0011 +0.1875f 0100 +0.2500f 0101 +0.3125f 0110 +0.3750f 0111 +0.4375f
pseudocode:
declare CVT_OFF_TABLE : 32'F[16];
  D0.f = CVT_OFF_TABLE[S0.u[3 : 0]]

name: V_CVT_F32_F64
description: Convert from a double-precision float input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_F64_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to a double-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_F32_UBYTE0
description: Convert an unsigned byte in byte 0 of the input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_CVT_F32_UBYTE1
description: Convert an unsigned byte in byte 1 of the input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_CVT_F32_UBYTE2
description: Convert an unsigned byte in byte 2 of the input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_CVT_F32_UBYTE3
description: Convert an unsigned byte in byte 3 of the input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_CVT_U32_F64
description: Convert from a double-precision float input to an unsigned 32-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, out-of-range floating point values (including infinity) saturate. NAN is converted to 0.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_F64_U32
description: Convert from an unsigned 32-bit integer input to a double-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0ULP accuracy.

name: V_TRUNC_F64
description: Compute the integer part of a double-precision float input with round-toward-zero semantics and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f64 = trunc(S0.f64)

name: V_CEIL_F64
description: Round the double-precision float input up to next integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.

name: V_RNDNE_F64
description: Round the double-precision float input to the nearest even integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f64 = floor(S0.f64 + 0.5);
  if (isEven(floor(S0.f64)) && (fract(S0.f64) == 0.5)) then
        D0.f64 -= 1.0
  endif

name: V_FLOOR_F64
description: Round the double-precision float input down to previous integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.

name: V_PIPEFLUSH
description: Flush the VALU destination cache.

name: V_MOV_B16
description: Move data to a VGPR.
notes:
Floating-point modifiers are valid for this instruction if S0.u16 is a 16-bit floating point value. This instruction is
suitable for negating or taking the absolute value of a floating-point value.

name: V_FRACT_F32
description: Compute the fractional portion of a single-precision float input and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S0.f + -floor(S0.f)
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are accepted.

This is intended to comply with the DX specification of fract where the function behaves like an extension of
integer modulus; be aware this may differ from how fract() is defined in other domains. For example: fract(-
1.2) = 0.8 in DX.

Obey round mode, result clamped to 0x3f7fffff.

name: V_TRUNC_F32
description: Compute the integer part of a single-precision float input with round-toward-zero semantics and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = trunc(S0.f)

name: V_CEIL_F32
description: Round the single-precision float input up to next integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f = trunc(S0.f);
  if ((S0.f > 0.0F) && (S0.f != D0.f)) then
        D0.f += 1.0F
  endif

name: V_RNDNE_F32
description: Round the single-precision float input to the nearest even integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f = floor(S0.f + 0.5F);
  if (isEven(64'F(floor(S0.f))) && (fract(S0.f) == 0.5F)) then

        D0.f -= 1.0F
  endif

name: V_FLOOR_F32
description: Round the single-precision float input down to previous integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f = trunc(S0.f);
  if ((S0.f < 0.0F) && (S0.f != D0.f)) then
        D0.f += -1.0F
  endif

name: V_EXP_F32
description: Calculate 2 raised to the power of the single-precision float input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = pow(2.0F, S0.f)
notes:
1ULP accuracy, denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_EXP_F32(0xff800000) => 0x00000000        // exp(-INF) = 0
V_EXP_F32(0x80000000) => 0x3f800000        // exp(-0.0) = 1
V_EXP_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x7f800000        // exp(+INF) = +INF

name: V_LOG_F32
description: Calculate the base 2 logarithm of the single-precision float input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = log2(S0.f)
notes:
1ULP accuracy, denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_LOG_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // log(-INF) = NAN
V_LOG_F32(0xbf800000) => 0xffc00000       // log(-1.0) = NAN
V_LOG_F32(0x80000000) => 0xff800000       // log(-0.0) = -INF
V_LOG_F32(0x00000000) => 0xff800000       // log(+0.0) = -INF
V_LOG_F32(0x3f800000) => 0x00000000       // log(+1.0) = 0
V_LOG_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x7f800000       // log(+INF) = +INF

name: V_RCP_F32
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the single-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy. Accuracy converges to < 0.5ULP when using the Newton-Raphson method and 2 FMA
operations. Denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_RCP_F32(0xff800000) => 0x80000000       // rcp(-INF) = -0
V_RCP_F32(0xc0000000) => 0xbf000000       // rcp(-2.0) = -0.5
V_RCP_F32(0x80000000) => 0xff800000       // rcp(-0.0) = -INF
V_RCP_F32(0x00000000) => 0x7f800000       // rcp(+0.0) = +INF
V_RCP_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x00000000       // rcp(+INF) = +0

name: V_RCP_IFLAG_F32
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the vector float input in a manner suitable for integer division and store the result into a vector register. This opcode is intended for use as part of an integer division macro.
pseudocode:
D0.f = 1.0F / S0.f;
  // Can only raise integer DIV_BY_ZERO exception
notes:
Can raise integer DIV_BY_ZERO exception but cannot raise floating-point exceptions. To be used in an integer
reciprocal macro by the compiler with one of the sequences listed below (depending on signed or unsigned
operation).

Unsigned usage:
CVT_F32_U32
RCP_IFLAG_F32

MUL_F32 (2**32 - 1)
CVT_U32_F32

Signed usage:
CVT_F32_I32
RCP_IFLAG_F32
MUL_F32 (2**31 - 1)
CVT_I32_F32

name: V_RSQ_F32
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the square root of the single-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = 1.0F / sqrt(S0.f)
notes:
1ULP accuracy, denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_RSQ_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // rsq(-INF) = NAN
V_RSQ_F32(0x80000000) => 0xff800000       // rsq(-0.0) = -INF
V_RSQ_F32(0x00000000) => 0x7f800000       // rsq(+0.0) = +INF
V_RSQ_F32(0x40800000) => 0x3f000000       // rsq(+4.0) = +0.5
V_RSQ_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x00000000       // rsq(+INF) = +0

name: V_RCP_F64
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the double-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes: This opcode has (2**29)ULP accuracy and supports denormals.

name: V_RSQ_F64
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the square root of the double-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes: This opcode has (2**29)ULP accuracy and supports denormals.

name: V_SQRT_F32
description: Calculate the square root of the single-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = sqrt(S0.f)
notes:
1ULP accuracy, denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_SQRT_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // sqrt(-INF) = NAN
V_SQRT_F32(0x80000000) => 0x80000000       // sqrt(-0.0) = -0
V_SQRT_F32(0x00000000) => 0x00000000       // sqrt(+0.0) = +0
V_SQRT_F32(0x40800000) => 0x40000000       // sqrt(+4.0) = +2.0
V_SQRT_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x7f800000       // sqrt(+INF) = +INF

name: V_SQRT_F64
description: Calculate the square root of the double-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f64 = sqrt(S0.f64)
notes: This opcode has (2**29)ULP accuracy and supports denormals.

name: V_SIN_F32
description: Calculate the trigonometric sine of a single-precision float value using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register. The operand is calculated by scaling the vector input by 2 PI.
notes:
Denormals are supported. Full range input is supported.

Functional examples:

V_SIN_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // sin(-INF) = NAN
V_SIN_F32(0xff7fffff) => 0x00000000       // -MaxFloat, finite
V_SIN_F32(0x80000000) => 0x80000000       // sin(-0.0) = -0
V_SIN_F32(0x3e800000) => 0x3f800000       // sin(0.25) = 1
V_SIN_F32(0x7f800000) => 0xffc00000       // sin(+INF) = NAN

name: V_COS_F32
description: Calculate the trigonometric cosine of a single-precision float value using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register. The operand is calculated by scaling the vector input by 2 PI.
notes:
Denormals are supported. Full range input is supported.

Functional examples:

V_COS_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // cos(-INF) = NAN
V_COS_F32(0xff7fffff) => 0x3f800000       // -MaxFloat, finite
V_COS_F32(0x80000000) => 0x3f800000       // cos(-0.0) = 1
V_COS_F32(0x3e800000) => 0x00000000       // cos(0.25) = 0
V_COS_F32(0x7f800000) => 0xffc00000       // cos(+INF) = NAN

name: V_NOT_B32
description: Calculate bitwise negation on a vector input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u = ~S0.u
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_BFREV_B32
description: Reverse the order of bits in a vector input and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_CLZ_I32_U32
description: Count the number of leading "0" bits before the first "1" in a vector input and store the result into a vector register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits.
notes:
Compare with S_CLZ_I32_U32, which performs the equivalent operation in the scalar ALU.

Functional examples:

V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x800000ff) => 0
V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x100000ff) => 3
V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x0000ffff) => 16
V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x00000001) => 31

name: V_CTZ_I32_B32
description: Count the number of trailing "0" bits before the first "1" in a vector input and store the result into a vector register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits in the input.
notes:
Compare with S_CTZ_I32_B32, which performs the equivalent operation in the scalar ALU.

Functional examples:

V_CTZ_I32_B32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
V_CTZ_I32_B32(0xff000001) => 0
V_CTZ_I32_B32(0xff000008) => 3
V_CTZ_I32_B32(0xffff0000) => 16
V_CTZ_I32_B32(0x80000000) => 31

name: V_CLS_I32
description: Count the number of leading bits that are the same as the sign bit of a vector input and store the result into a vector register. Store -1 if all input bits are the same.
notes:
Compare with S_CLS_I32, which performs the equivalent operation in the scalar ALU.

Functional examples:

V_CLS_I32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
V_CLS_I32(0x40000000) => 1
V_CLS_I32(0x80000000) => 1
V_CLS_I32(0x0fffffff) => 4
V_CLS_I32(0xffff0000) => 16
V_CLS_I32(0xfffffffe) => 31

V_CLS_I32(0xffffffff) => 0xffffffff

name: V_FREXP_EXP_I32_F64
description: Extract the exponent of a double-precision float input and store the result as a signed 32-bit integer into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((S0.f64 == +INF) || (S0.f64 == -INF) || isNAN(S0.f64)) then
        D0.i = 0
  else
        D0.i = exponent(S0.f64) - 1023 + 1
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f64 = significand * (2 ** exponent). See also V_FREXP_MANT_F64,
which returns the significand. See the C library function frexp() for more information.

name: V_FREXP_MANT_F64
description: Extract the binary significand, or mantissa, of a double-precision float input and store the result as a double- precision float into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((S0.f64 == +INF) || (S0.f64 == -INF) || isNAN(S0.f64)) then
        D0.f64 = S0.f64
  else
        D0.f64 = mantissa(S0.f64)
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f64 = significand * (2 ** exponent). Result range is in (-1.0,-0.5][0.5,1.0)
in normal cases. See also V_FREXP_EXP_I_F64, which returns integer exponent. See the C library function
frexp() for more information.

name: V_FRACT_F64
description: Compute the fractional portion of a double-precision float input and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are accepted.

This is intended to comply with the DX specification of fract where the function behaves like an extension of
integer modulus; be aware this may differ from how fract() is defined in other domains. For example: fract(-
1.2) = 0.8 in DX.

Obey round mode, result clamped to 0x3fefffffffffffff.

name: V_FREXP_EXP_I32_F32
description: Extract the exponent of a single-precision float input and store the result as a signed 32-bit integer into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f) == +INF) || (64'F(S0.f) == -INF) || isNAN(64'F(S0.f))) then
        D0.i = 0
  else
        D0.i = exponent(S0.f) - 127 + 1
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f32 = significand * (2 ** exponent). See also V_FREXP_MANT_F32,
which returns the significand. See the C library function frexp() for more information.

name: V_FREXP_MANT_F32
description: Extract the binary significand, or mantissa, of a single-precision float input and store the result as a single- precision float into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f) == +INF) || (64'F(S0.f) == -INF) || isNAN(64'F(S0.f))) then
        D0.f = S0.f
  else
        D0.f = mantissa(S0.f)
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f32 = significand * (2 ** exponent). Result range is in (-1.0,-0.5][0.5,1.0)
in normal cases. See also V_FREXP_EXP_I_F32, which returns integer exponent. See the C library function
frexp() for more information.

name: V_MOVRELD_B32
description: Move to a relative destination address.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move v15 <= v7:

s_mov_b32 m0, 10
v_movreld_b32 v5, v7

name: V_MOVRELS_B32
description: Move from a relative source address.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move v5 <= v17:

s_mov_b32 m0, 10
v_movrels_b32 v5, v7

name: V_MOVRELSD_B32
description: Move from a relative source address to a relative destination address.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move v15 <= v17:

s_mov_b32 m0, 10
v_movrelsd_b32 v5, v7

name: V_MOVRELSD_2_B32
description: Move from a relative source address to a relative destination address, with different relative offsets.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move v25 <= v17:

s_mov_b32 m0, ((20 << 16) | 10)
v_movrelsd_2_b32 v5, v7

name: V_CVT_F16_U16
description: Convert from an unsigned 16-bit integer input to an FP16 float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, supports denormals, rounding, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_CVT_F16_I16
description: Convert from a signed 16-bit integer input to an FP16 float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, supports denormals, rounding, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_CVT_U16_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to an unsigned 16-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy, supports rounding, exception flags and saturation. FP16 denormals are accepted. Conversion
is done with truncation.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_I16_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to a signed 16-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy, supports rounding, exception flags and saturation. FP16 denormals are accepted. Conversion
is done with truncation.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_RCP_F16
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the half-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy.

Functional examples:

V_RCP_F16(0xfc00) => 0x8000          // rcp(-INF) = -0
V_RCP_F16(0xc000) => 0xb800          // rcp(-2.0) = -0.5
V_RCP_F16(0x8000) => 0xfc00          // rcp(-0.0) = -INF
V_RCP_F16(0x0000) => 0x7c00          // rcp(+0.0) = +INF
V_RCP_F16(0x7c00) => 0x0000          // rcp(+INF) = +0

name: V_SQRT_F16
description: Calculate the square root of the half-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f16 = sqrt(S0.f16)
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

Functional examples:

V_SQRT_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00         // sqrt(-INF) = NAN
V_SQRT_F16(0x8000) => 0x8000         // sqrt(-0.0) = -0
V_SQRT_F16(0x0000) => 0x0000         // sqrt(+0.0) = +0
V_SQRT_F16(0x4400) => 0x4000         // sqrt(+4.0) = +2.0
V_SQRT_F16(0x7c00) => 0x7c00         // sqrt(+INF) = +INF

name: V_RSQ_F16
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the square root of the half-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

Functional examples:

V_RSQ_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00          // rsq(-INF) = NAN
V_RSQ_F16(0x8000) => 0xfc00          // rsq(-0.0) = -INF
V_RSQ_F16(0x0000) => 0x7c00          // rsq(+0.0) = +INF

V_RSQ_F16(0x4400) => 0x3800          // rsq(+4.0) = +0.5
V_RSQ_F16(0x7c00) => 0x0000          // rsq(+INF) = +0

name: V_LOG_F16
description: Calculate the base 2 logarithm of the half-precision float input and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

Functional examples:

V_LOG_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00          // log(-INF) = NAN
V_LOG_F16(0xbc00) => 0xfe00          // log(-1.0) = NAN
V_LOG_F16(0x8000) => 0xfc00          // log(-0.0) = -INF
V_LOG_F16(0x0000) => 0xfc00          // log(+0.0) = -INF
V_LOG_F16(0x3c00) => 0x0000          // log(+1.0) = 0
V_LOG_F16(0x7c00) => 0x7c00          // log(+INF) = +INF

name: V_EXP_F16
description: Calculate 2 raised to the power of the half-precision float input and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

Functional examples:

V_EXP_F16(0xfc00) => 0x0000          // exp(-INF) = 0
V_EXP_F16(0x8000) => 0x3c00          // exp(-0.0) = 1
V_EXP_F16(0x7c00) => 0x7c00          // exp(+INF) = +INF

name: V_FREXP_MANT_F16
description: Extract the binary significand, or mantissa, of an FP16 float input and store the result as an FP16 float into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f16) == +INF) || (64'F(S0.f16) == -INF) || isNAN(64'F(S0.f16))) then
        D0.f16 = S0.f16
  else
        D0.f16 = mantissa(S0.f16)
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f16 = significand * (2 ** exponent). Result range is in (-1.0,-0.5][0.5,1.0)
in normal cases. See also V_FREXP_EXP_I_F16, which returns integer exponent. See the C library function
frexp() for more information.

name: V_FREXP_EXP_I16_F16
description: Extract the exponent of an FP16 float input and store the result as a signed 16-bit integer into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f16) == +INF) || (64'F(S0.f16) == -INF) || isNAN(64'F(S0.f16))) then
        D0.i16 = 16'0
  else
        D0.i16 = 16'I(exponent(S0.f16) - 15 + 1)
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f16 = significand * (2 ** exponent). See also V_FREXP_MANT_F16,
which returns the significand. See the C library function frexp() for more information.

name: V_FLOOR_F16
description: Round the half-precision float input down to previous integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.

name: V_CEIL_F16
description: Round the half-precision float input up to next integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.

name: V_TRUNC_F16
description: Compute the integer part of an FP16 float input with round-toward-zero semantics and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f16 = trunc(S0.f16)

name: V_RNDNE_F16
description: Round the half-precision float input to the nearest even integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f16 = floor(S0.f16 + 16'0.5);
  if (isEven(64'F(floor(S0.f16))) && (fract(S0.f16) == 16'0.5)) then
        D0.f16 -= 16'1.0
  endif

name: V_FRACT_F16
description: Compute the fractional portion of an FP16 float input and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are accepted.

This is intended to comply with the DX specification of fract where the function behaves like an extension of
integer modulus; be aware this may differ from how fract() is defined in other domains. For example: fract(-
1.2) = 0.8 in DX.

name: V_SIN_F16
description: Calculate the trigonometric sine of an FP16 float value using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register. The operand is calculated by scaling the vector input by 2 PI.
notes:
Denormals are supported. Full range input is supported.

Functional examples:

V_SIN_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00          // sin(-INF) = NAN
V_SIN_F16(0xfbff) => 0x0000          // Most negative finite FP16
V_SIN_F16(0x8000) => 0x8000          // sin(-0.0) = -0
V_SIN_F16(0x3400) => 0x3c00          // sin(0.25) = 1
V_SIN_F16(0x7bff) => 0x0000          // Most positive finite FP16
V_SIN_F16(0x7c00) => 0xfe00          // sin(+INF) = NAN

name: V_COS_F16
description: Calculate the trigonometric cosine of an FP16 float value using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register. The operand is calculated by scaling the vector input by 2 PI.
notes:
Denormals are supported. Full range input is supported.

Functional examples:

V_COS_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00          // cos(-INF) = NAN
V_COS_F16(0xfbff) => 0x3c00          // Most negative finite FP16
V_COS_F16(0x8000) => 0x3c00          // cos(-0.0) = 1
V_COS_F16(0x3400) => 0x0000          // cos(0.25) = 0
V_COS_F16(0x7bff) => 0x3c00          // Most positive finite FP16
V_COS_F16(0x7c00) => 0xfe00          // cos(+INF) = NAN

name: V_SAT_PK_U8_I16
description: Given two 16-bit unsigned integer inputs, saturate each input over an 8-bit unsigned range, pack the resulting values into a 16-bit word and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
SAT8 = lambda(n) (
        if n.i <= 0 then
            return 8'0U
        elsif n >= 16'I(0xff) then
            return 8'255U
        else
            return n[7 : 0].u8
        endif);
  D0.b16 = { SAT8(S0[31 : 16].i16), SAT8(S0[15 : 0].i16) }
notes: Used for 4x16bit data packed as 4x8bit data.

name: V_CVT_NORM_I16_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to a signed normalized short and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i16 = f16_to_snorm(S0.f16)
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, supports rounding, exception flags and saturation, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_NORM_U16_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to an unsigned normalized short and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u16 = f16_to_unorm(S0.f16)
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, supports rounding, exception flags and saturation, denormals are supported.

name: V_SWAP_B32
description: Swap the values in two vector registers.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported; this is an untyped operation.

name: V_SWAP_B16
description: Swap the values in two vector registers.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported; this is an untyped operation.

name: V_PERMLANE64_B32
description: Perform a specific permutation across lanes where the high half and low half of a wave64 are swapped. Performs no operation in wave32 mode.
notes:
In wave32 mode this opcode is translated to V_NOP and performs no writes.

In wave64 the EXEC mask of the destination lane is used as the read mask for the alternate lane; as a result this
opcode may read values from disabled lanes.

The source must be a VGPR and SVGPRs are not allowed for this opcode.

ABS, NEG and OMOD modifiers should all be zeroed for this instruction.

name: V_SWAPREL_B32
description: Swap values of two operands. The two addresses are relatively indexed using M0.
notes:
Input and output modifiers not supported; this is an untyped operation.

Example: The following instruction sequence swaps v25 and v17:

s_mov_b32 m0, ((20 << 16) | 10)
v_swaprel_b32 v5, v7

name: V_NOT_B16
description: Calculate bitwise negation on a vector input and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_CVT_I32_I16
description: Convert from an 16-bit signed integer to a 32-bit signed integer, sign extending as needed.
pseudocode: D0.i = 32'I(signext(S0.i16))
notes: To convert in the other direction (from 32-bit to 16-bit integer) use V_MOV_B16.

name: V_CVT_U32_U16
description: Convert from an 16-bit unsigned integer to a 32-bit unsigned integer, zero extending as needed.
notes:
To convert in the other direction (from 32-bit to 16-bit integer) use V_MOV_B16.

16.8.1. VOP1 using VOP3 encoding
Instructions in this format may also be encoded as VOP3. VOP3 allows access to the extra control bits (e.g. ABS,
OMOD) at the expense of a larger instruction word. The VOP3 opcode is: VOP2 opcode + 0x180.

name: V_CMP_CLASS_F16
description: IEEE numeric class function specified in S1.u, performed on S0.f16. The function reports true if the floating point value is any of the numeric types selected in S1.u according to the following list: S1.u[0] -- value is a signaling NAN. S1.u[1] -- value is a quiet NAN. S1.u[2] -- value is negative infinity. S1.u[3] -- value is a negative normal value. S1.u[4] -- value is a negative denormal value. S1.u[5] -- value is negative zero. S1.u[6] -- value is positive zero. S1.u[7] -- value is a positive denormal value. S1.u[8] -- value is a positive normal value. S1.u[9] -- value is positive infinity.
notes: Signal 'invalid' on sNAN's, and also on qNAN's if clamp is set.

name: V_CMP_CLASS_F32
description: IEEE numeric class function specified in S1.u, performed on S0.f. The function reports true if the floating point value is any of the numeric types selected in S1.u according to the following list: S1.u[0] -- value is a signaling NAN. S1.u[1] -- value is a quiet NAN. S1.u[2] -- value is negative infinity. S1.u[3] -- value is a negative normal value. S1.u[4] -- value is a negative denormal value. S1.u[5] -- value is negative zero. S1.u[6] -- value is positive zero. S1.u[7] -- value is a positive denormal value. S1.u[8] -- value is a positive normal value. S1.u[9] -- value is positive infinity.

S1.u[6] -- value is positive zero. S1.u[7] -- value is a positive denormal value. S1.u[8] -- value is a positive normal value. S1.u[9] -- value is positive infinity.

notes: Write only EXEC. SDST must be set to EXEC_LO. Signal 'invalid' on sNAN's, and also on qNAN's if clamp is set.

name: V_CMPX_CLASS_F16
description: IEEE numeric class function specified in S1.u, performed on S0.f16. The function reports true if the floating point value is any of the numeric types selected in S1.u according to the following list: S1.u[0] -- value is a signaling NAN. S1.u[1] -- value is a quiet NAN. S1.u[2] -- value is negative infinity. S1.u[3] -- value is a negative normal value. S1.u[4] -- value is a negative denormal value. S1.u[5] -- value is negative zero. S1.u[6] -- value is positive zero. S1.u[7] -- value is a positive denormal value. S1.u[8] -- value is a positive normal value. S1.u[9] -- value is positive infinity.
notes: Write only EXEC. SDST must be set to EXEC_LO. Signal 'invalid' on sNAN's, and also on qNAN's if clamp is set.

name: V_CMPX_CLASS_F32
description: IEEE numeric class function specified in S1.u, performed on S0.f. The function reports true if the floating point value is any of the numeric types selected in S1.u according to the following list: S1.u[0] -- value is a signaling NAN. S1.u[1] -- value is a quiet NAN. S1.u[2] -- value is negative infinity. S1.u[3] -- value is a negative normal value. S1.u[4] -- value is a negative denormal value. S1.u[5] -- value is negative zero. S1.u[6] -- value is positive zero. S1.u[7] -- value is a positive denormal value. S1.u[8] -- value is a positive normal value. S1.u[9] -- value is positive infinity.
notes: Write only EXEC. SDST must be set to EXEC_LO. Signal 'invalid' on sNAN's, and also on qNAN's if clamp is set.

name: V_CMPX_CLASS_F64
description: IEEE numeric class function specified in S1.u, performed on S0.f64. The function reports true if the floating point value is any of the numeric types selected in S1.u according to the following list: S1.u[0] -- value is a signaling NAN. S1.u[1] -- value is a quiet NAN. S1.u[2] -- value is negative infinity. S1.u[3] -- value is a negative normal value. S1.u[4] -- value is a negative denormal value. S1.u[5] -- value is negative zero. S1.u[6] -- value is positive zero. S1.u[7] -- value is a positive denormal value. S1.u[8] -- value is a positive normal value. S1.u[9] -- value is positive infinity.
notes:
Write only EXEC. SDST must be set to EXEC_LO. Signal 'invalid' on sNAN's, and also on qNAN's if clamp is set.

16.9.1. VOPC using VOP3 encoding
Instructions in this format may also be encoded as VOP3. VOP3 allows access to the extra control bits (e.g. ABS,
OMOD) at the expense of a larger instruction word. The VOP3 opcode is: VOP2 opcode + 0x000.

When the CLAMP microcode bit is set to 1, these compare instructions signal an exception when either of the
inputs is NaN. When CLAMP is set to zero, NaN does not signal an exception. The second eight VOPC
instructions have {OP8} embedded in them. This refers to each of the compare operations listed below.

VDST    = Destination for instruction in the VGPR.
ABS     = Floating-point absolute value.
CLMP    = Clamp output.
OP      = Instruction opcode.
SRC0    = First operand for instruction.
SRC1    = Second operand for instruction.
SRC2    = Third operand for instruction. Unused in VOPC instructions.
OMOD    = Output modifier for instruction. Unused in VOPC instructions.
NEG     = Floating-point negation.

name: V_FMA_MIX_F32
description: Fused-multiply-add of single-precision values with MIX encoding. Size and location of S0, S1 and S2 controlled by OPSEL: 0=src[31:0], 1=src[31:0], 2=src[15:0], 3=src[31:16]. Also, for FMA_MIX, the NEG_HI field acts instead as an absolute-value modifier.
pseudocode:
declare in : 32'F[3];
  declare S : 32'B[3];
  for i in 0 : 2 do
      if !OPSEL_HI.u3[i] then
            in[i] = S[i].f
      elsif OPSEL.u3[i] then
            in[i] = f16_to_f32(S[i][31 : 16].f16)
      else
            in[i] = f16_to_f32(S[i][15 : 0].f16)
      endif
  endfor;
  D0[31 : 0].f = fma(in[0], in[1], in[2])

name: V_FMA_MIXLO_F16
description: Fused-multiply-add of FP16 values with MIX encoding, result stored in low 16 bits of destination. Size and location of S0, S1 and S2 controlled by OPSEL: 0=src[31:0], 1=src[31:0], 2=src[15:0], 3=src[31:16]. Also, for FMA_MIX, the NEG_HI field acts instead as an absolute-value modifier.
pseudocode:
declare in : 32'F[3];
  declare S : 32'B[3];
  for i in 0 : 2 do
      if !OPSEL_HI.u3[i] then
            in[i] = S[i].f
      elsif OPSEL.u3[i] then
            in[i] = f16_to_f32(S[i][31 : 16].f16)
      else
            in[i] = f16_to_f32(S[i][15 : 0].f16)
      endif
  endfor;
  D0[15 : 0].f16 = f32_to_f16(fma(in[0], in[1], in[2]))

name: V_FMA_MIXHI_F16
description: Fused-multiply-add of FP16 values with MIX encoding, result stored in HIGH 16 bits of destination. Size and location of S0, S1 and S2 controlled by OPSEL: 0=src[31:0], 1=src[31:0], 2=src[15:0], 3=src[31:16]. Also, for FMA_MIX, the NEG_HI field acts instead as an absolute-value modifier.
pseudocode:
declare in : 32'F[3];

      endif

  D0[31 : 16].f16 = f32_to_f16(fma(in[0], in[1], in[2]))

name: V_MOV_B32

pseudocode: D0.b = S0.b
notes:
Floating-point modifiers are valid for this instruction if S0.u is a 32-bit floating point value. This instruction is
suitable for negating or taking the absolute value of a floating-point value.

Functional examples:

v_mov_b32 v0, v1    // Move v1 to v0
v_mov_b32 v0, -v1   // Set v1 to the negation of v0
v_mov_b32 v0, abs(v1)   // Set v1 to the absolute value of v0

name: V_READFIRSTLANE_B32
description: Read the scalar value in the lowest active lane of the input vector register and store it into a scalar register.
notes:
Overrides EXEC mask for the VGPR read. Input and output modifiers not supported; this is an untyped
operation.

name: V_CVT_I32_F64
description: Convert from a double-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, out-of-range floating point values (including infinity) saturate. NAN is converted to 0.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_F64_I32
description: Convert from a signed 32-bit integer input to a double-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0ULP accuracy.

name: V_CVT_F32_I32
description: Convert from a signed 32-bit integer input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy.

name: V_CVT_F32_U32
description: Convert from an unsigned 32-bit integer input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy.

name: V_CVT_U32_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to an unsigned 32-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy, out-of-range floating point values (including infinity) saturate. NAN is converted to 0.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this

conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_I32_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy, out-of-range floating point values (including infinity) saturate. NAN is converted to 0.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_F16_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to an FP16 float and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, supports input modifiers and creates FP16 denormals when appropriate. Flush denorms on
output if specified based on DP denorm mode. Output rounding based on DP rounding mode.

name: V_CVT_F32_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0ULP accuracy, FP16 denormal inputs are accepted. Flush denorms on input if specified based on DP denorm
mode.

name: V_CVT_NEAREST_I32_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer using round-to-nearest-integer semantics (ignore the default rounding mode) and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i = f32_to_i32(floor(S0.f + 0.5F))
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_FLOOR_I32_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer using round-down semantics (ignore the default rounding mode) and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i = f32_to_i32(floor(S0.f))
notes: 1ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_OFF_F32_I4
description: Convert from a signed 4-bit integer to a single-precision float using an offset table and store the result into a vector register. Used for interpolation in shader. Lookup table on S0[3:0]: S0 binary Result 1000 -0.5000f 1001 -0.4375f 1010 -0.3750f 1011 -0.3125f 1100 -0.2500f 1101 -0.1875f 1110 -0.1250f 1111 -0.0625f 0000 +0.0000f 0001 +0.0625f 0010 +0.1250f 0011 +0.1875f 0100 +0.2500f 0101 +0.3125f 0110 +0.3750f 0111 +0.4375f
pseudocode:
declare CVT_OFF_TABLE : 32'F[16];
  D0.f = CVT_OFF_TABLE[S0.u[3 : 0]]

name: V_CVT_F32_F64
description: Convert from a double-precision float input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_F64_F32
description: Convert from a single-precision float input to a double-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_F32_UBYTE0
description: Convert an unsigned byte in byte 0 of the input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_CVT_F32_UBYTE1
description: Convert an unsigned byte in byte 1 of the input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_CVT_F32_UBYTE2
description: Convert an unsigned byte in byte 2 of the input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_CVT_F32_UBYTE3
description: Convert an unsigned byte in byte 3 of the input to a single-precision float and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_CVT_U32_F64
description: Convert from a double-precision float input to an unsigned 32-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, out-of-range floating point values (including infinity) saturate. NAN is converted to 0.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_F64_U32
description: Convert from an unsigned 32-bit integer input to a double-precision float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0ULP accuracy.

name: V_TRUNC_F64
description: Compute the integer part of a double-precision float input with round-toward-zero semantics and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f64 = trunc(S0.f64)

name: V_CEIL_F64
description: Round the double-precision float input up to next integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.

name: V_RNDNE_F64
description: Round the double-precision float input to the nearest even integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f64 = floor(S0.f64 + 0.5);
  if (isEven(floor(S0.f64)) && (fract(S0.f64) == 0.5)) then
      D0.f64 -= 1.0
  endif

name: V_FLOOR_F64
description: Round the double-precision float input down to previous integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.

name: V_PIPEFLUSH
description: Flush the VALU destination cache.

name: V_MOV_B16
description: Move data to a VGPR.
notes:
Floating-point modifiers are valid for this instruction if S0.u16 is a 16-bit floating point value. This instruction is
suitable for negating or taking the absolute value of a floating-point value.

name: V_FRACT_F32
description: Compute the fractional portion of a single-precision float input and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S0.f + -floor(S0.f)
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are accepted.

This is intended to comply with the DX specification of fract where the function behaves like an extension of
integer modulus; be aware this may differ from how fract() is defined in other domains. For example: fract(-
1.2) = 0.8 in DX.

Obey round mode, result clamped to 0x3f7fffff.

name: V_TRUNC_F32
description: Compute the integer part of a single-precision float input with round-toward-zero semantics and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = trunc(S0.f)

name: V_CEIL_F32
description: Round the single-precision float input up to next integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f = trunc(S0.f);
  if ((S0.f > 0.0F) && (S0.f != D0.f)) then

        D0.f += 1.0F
  endif

name: V_RNDNE_F32
description: Round the single-precision float input to the nearest even integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f = floor(S0.f + 0.5F);
  if (isEven(64'F(floor(S0.f))) && (fract(S0.f) == 0.5F)) then
        D0.f -= 1.0F
  endif

name: V_FLOOR_F32
description: Round the single-precision float input down to previous integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f = trunc(S0.f);
  if ((S0.f < 0.0F) && (S0.f != D0.f)) then
        D0.f += -1.0F
  endif

name: V_EXP_F32
description: Calculate 2 raised to the power of the single-precision float input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = pow(2.0F, S0.f)
notes:
1ULP accuracy, denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_EXP_F32(0xff800000) => 0x00000000        // exp(-INF) = 0
V_EXP_F32(0x80000000) => 0x3f800000        // exp(-0.0) = 1
V_EXP_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x7f800000        // exp(+INF) = +INF

name: V_LOG_F32
description: Calculate the base 2 logarithm of the single-precision float input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = log2(S0.f)
notes:
1ULP accuracy, denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_LOG_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // log(-INF) = NAN
V_LOG_F32(0xbf800000) => 0xffc00000       // log(-1.0) = NAN
V_LOG_F32(0x80000000) => 0xff800000       // log(-0.0) = -INF
V_LOG_F32(0x00000000) => 0xff800000       // log(+0.0) = -INF
V_LOG_F32(0x3f800000) => 0x00000000       // log(+1.0) = 0
V_LOG_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x7f800000       // log(+INF) = +INF

name: V_RCP_F32
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the single-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy. Accuracy converges to < 0.5ULP when using the Newton-Raphson method and 2 FMA
operations. Denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_RCP_F32(0xff800000) => 0x80000000       // rcp(-INF) = -0
V_RCP_F32(0xc0000000) => 0xbf000000       // rcp(-2.0) = -0.5
V_RCP_F32(0x80000000) => 0xff800000       // rcp(-0.0) = -INF
V_RCP_F32(0x00000000) => 0x7f800000       // rcp(+0.0) = +INF
V_RCP_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x00000000       // rcp(+INF) = +0

name: V_RCP_IFLAG_F32
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the vector float input in a manner suitable for integer division and store the result into a vector register. This opcode is intended for use as part of an integer division macro.
pseudocode:
D0.f = 1.0F / S0.f;
  // Can only raise integer DIV_BY_ZERO exception
notes:
Can raise integer DIV_BY_ZERO exception but cannot raise floating-point exceptions. To be used in an integer
reciprocal macro by the compiler with one of the sequences listed below (depending on signed or unsigned
operation).

Unsigned usage:
CVT_F32_U32
RCP_IFLAG_F32
MUL_F32 (2**32 - 1)
CVT_U32_F32

Signed usage:
CVT_F32_I32
RCP_IFLAG_F32
MUL_F32 (2**31 - 1)
CVT_I32_F32

name: V_RSQ_F32
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the square root of the single-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = 1.0F / sqrt(S0.f)
notes:
1ULP accuracy, denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_RSQ_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // rsq(-INF) = NAN
V_RSQ_F32(0x80000000) => 0xff800000       // rsq(-0.0) = -INF
V_RSQ_F32(0x00000000) => 0x7f800000       // rsq(+0.0) = +INF
V_RSQ_F32(0x40800000) => 0x3f000000       // rsq(+4.0) = +0.5
V_RSQ_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x00000000       // rsq(+INF) = +0

name: V_RCP_F64
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the double-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes: This opcode has (2**29)ULP accuracy and supports denormals.

name: V_RSQ_F64
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the square root of the double-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes: This opcode has (2**29)ULP accuracy and supports denormals.

name: V_SQRT_F32
description: Calculate the square root of the single-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = sqrt(S0.f)
notes:
1ULP accuracy, denormals are flushed.

Functional examples:

V_SQRT_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // sqrt(-INF) = NAN
V_SQRT_F32(0x80000000) => 0x80000000       // sqrt(-0.0) = -0
V_SQRT_F32(0x00000000) => 0x00000000       // sqrt(+0.0) = +0
V_SQRT_F32(0x40800000) => 0x40000000       // sqrt(+4.0) = +2.0
V_SQRT_F32(0x7f800000) => 0x7f800000       // sqrt(+INF) = +INF

name: V_SQRT_F64
description: Calculate the square root of the double-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f64 = sqrt(S0.f64)
notes: This opcode has (2**29)ULP accuracy and supports denormals.

name: V_SIN_F32
description: Calculate the trigonometric sine of a single-precision float value using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register. The operand is calculated by scaling the vector input by 2 PI.
notes:
Denormals are supported. Full range input is supported.

Functional examples:

V_SIN_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // sin(-INF) = NAN
V_SIN_F32(0xff7fffff) => 0x00000000       // -MaxFloat, finite
V_SIN_F32(0x80000000) => 0x80000000       // sin(-0.0) = -0
V_SIN_F32(0x3e800000) => 0x3f800000       // sin(0.25) = 1
V_SIN_F32(0x7f800000) => 0xffc00000       // sin(+INF) = NAN

name: V_COS_F32
description: Calculate the trigonometric cosine of a single-precision float value using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register. The operand is calculated by scaling the vector input by 2 PI.
notes:
Denormals are supported. Full range input is supported.

Functional examples:

V_COS_F32(0xff800000) => 0xffc00000       // cos(-INF) = NAN
V_COS_F32(0xff7fffff) => 0x3f800000       // -MaxFloat, finite
V_COS_F32(0x80000000) => 0x3f800000       // cos(-0.0) = 1
V_COS_F32(0x3e800000) => 0x00000000       // cos(0.25) = 0

V_COS_F32(0x7f800000) => 0xffc00000        // cos(+INF) = NAN

name: V_NOT_B32
description: Calculate bitwise negation on a vector input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u = ~S0.u
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_BFREV_B32
description: Reverse the order of bits in a vector input and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_CLZ_I32_U32
description: Count the number of leading "0" bits before the first "1" in a vector input and store the result into a vector register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits.
notes:
Compare with S_CLZ_I32_U32, which performs the equivalent operation in the scalar ALU.

Functional examples:

V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x800000ff) => 0
V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x100000ff) => 3
V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x0000ffff) => 16
V_CLZ_I32_U32(0x00000001) => 31

name: V_CTZ_I32_B32
description: Count the number of trailing "0" bits before the first "1" in a vector input and store the result into a vector register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits in the input.
notes:
Compare with S_CTZ_I32_B32, which performs the equivalent operation in the scalar ALU.

Functional examples:

V_CTZ_I32_B32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
V_CTZ_I32_B32(0xff000001) => 0
V_CTZ_I32_B32(0xff000008) => 3
V_CTZ_I32_B32(0xffff0000) => 16
V_CTZ_I32_B32(0x80000000) => 31

name: V_CLS_I32
description: Count the number of leading bits that are the same as the sign bit of a vector input and store the result into a vector register. Store -1 if all input bits are the same.
notes:
Compare with S_CLS_I32, which performs the equivalent operation in the scalar ALU.

Functional examples:

V_CLS_I32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
V_CLS_I32(0x40000000) => 1
V_CLS_I32(0x80000000) => 1
V_CLS_I32(0x0fffffff) => 4
V_CLS_I32(0xffff0000) => 16
V_CLS_I32(0xfffffffe) => 31
V_CLS_I32(0xffffffff) => 0xffffffff

name: V_FREXP_EXP_I32_F64
description: Extract the exponent of a double-precision float input and store the result as a signed 32-bit integer into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((S0.f64 == +INF) || (S0.f64 == -INF) || isNAN(S0.f64)) then
        D0.i = 0
  else
        D0.i = exponent(S0.f64) - 1023 + 1
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f64 = significand * (2 ** exponent). See also V_FREXP_MANT_F64,
which returns the significand. See the C library function frexp() for more information.

name: V_FREXP_MANT_F64
description: Extract the binary significand, or mantissa, of a double-precision float input and store the result as a double- precision float into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((S0.f64 == +INF) || (S0.f64 == -INF) || isNAN(S0.f64)) then
        D0.f64 = S0.f64
  else
        D0.f64 = mantissa(S0.f64)
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f64 = significand * (2 ** exponent). Result range is in (-1.0,-0.5][0.5,1.0)
in normal cases. See also V_FREXP_EXP_I_F64, which returns integer exponent. See the C library function
frexp() for more information.

name: V_FRACT_F64
description: Compute the fractional portion of a double-precision float input and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are accepted.

This is intended to comply with the DX specification of fract where the function behaves like an extension of
integer modulus; be aware this may differ from how fract() is defined in other domains. For example: fract(-
1.2) = 0.8 in DX.

Obey round mode, result clamped to 0x3fefffffffffffff.

name: V_FREXP_EXP_I32_F32
description: Extract the exponent of a single-precision float input and store the result as a signed 32-bit integer into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f) == +INF) || (64'F(S0.f) == -INF) || isNAN(64'F(S0.f))) then
        D0.i = 0
  else
        D0.i = exponent(S0.f) - 127 + 1
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f32 = significand * (2 ** exponent). See also V_FREXP_MANT_F32,
which returns the significand. See the C library function frexp() for more information.

name: V_FREXP_MANT_F32
description: Extract the binary significand, or mantissa, of a single-precision float input and store the result as a single- precision float into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f) == +INF) || (64'F(S0.f) == -INF) || isNAN(64'F(S0.f))) then
        D0.f = S0.f

  else
        D0.f = mantissa(S0.f)
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f32 = significand * (2 ** exponent). Result range is in (-1.0,-0.5][0.5,1.0)
in normal cases. See also V_FREXP_EXP_I_F32, which returns integer exponent. See the C library function
frexp() for more information.

name: V_MOVRELD_B32
description: Move to a relative destination address.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move v15 <= v7:

s_mov_b32 m0, 10
v_movreld_b32 v5, v7

name: V_MOVRELS_B32
description: Move from a relative source address.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move v5 <= v17:

s_mov_b32 m0, 10
v_movrels_b32 v5, v7

name: V_MOVRELSD_B32
description: Move from a relative source address to a relative destination address.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move v15 <= v17:

s_mov_b32 m0, 10
v_movrelsd_b32 v5, v7

name: V_MOVRELSD_2_B32
description: Move from a relative source address to a relative destination address, with different relative offsets.
notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move v25 <= v17:

s_mov_b32 m0, ((20 << 16) | 10)
v_movrelsd_2_b32 v5, v7

name: V_CVT_F16_U16
description: Convert from an unsigned 16-bit integer input to an FP16 float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, supports denormals, rounding, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_CVT_F16_I16
description: Convert from a signed 16-bit integer input to an FP16 float and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, supports denormals, rounding, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_CVT_U16_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to an unsigned 16-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy, supports rounding, exception flags and saturation. FP16 denormals are accepted. Conversion
is done with truncation.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_CVT_I16_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to a signed 16-bit integer and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
1ULP accuracy, supports rounding, exception flags and saturation. FP16 denormals are accepted. Conversion
is done with truncation.

Generation of the INEXACT exception is controlled by the CLAMP bit. INEXACT exceptions are enabled for this
conversion iff CLAMP == 1.

name: V_RCP_F16
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the half-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy.

Functional examples:

V_RCP_F16(0xfc00) => 0x8000          // rcp(-INF) = -0
V_RCP_F16(0xc000) => 0xb800          // rcp(-2.0) = -0.5
V_RCP_F16(0x8000) => 0xfc00          // rcp(-0.0) = -INF
V_RCP_F16(0x0000) => 0x7c00          // rcp(+0.0) = +INF
V_RCP_F16(0x7c00) => 0x0000          // rcp(+INF) = +0

name: V_SQRT_F16
description: Calculate the square root of the half-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f16 = sqrt(S0.f16)
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

Functional examples:

V_SQRT_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00         // sqrt(-INF) = NAN
V_SQRT_F16(0x8000) => 0x8000         // sqrt(-0.0) = -0
V_SQRT_F16(0x0000) => 0x0000         // sqrt(+0.0) = +0
V_SQRT_F16(0x4400) => 0x4000         // sqrt(+4.0) = +2.0
V_SQRT_F16(0x7c00) => 0x7c00         // sqrt(+INF) = +INF

name: V_RSQ_F16
description: Calculate the reciprocal of the square root of the half-precision float input using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

Functional examples:

V_RSQ_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00          // rsq(-INF) = NAN
V_RSQ_F16(0x8000) => 0xfc00          // rsq(-0.0) = -INF
V_RSQ_F16(0x0000) => 0x7c00          // rsq(+0.0) = +INF
V_RSQ_F16(0x4400) => 0x3800          // rsq(+4.0) = +0.5
V_RSQ_F16(0x7c00) => 0x0000          // rsq(+INF) = +0

name: V_LOG_F16
description: Calculate the base 2 logarithm of the half-precision float input and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

Functional examples:

V_LOG_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00          // log(-INF) = NAN
V_LOG_F16(0xbc00) => 0xfe00          // log(-1.0) = NAN
V_LOG_F16(0x8000) => 0xfc00          // log(-0.0) = -INF
V_LOG_F16(0x0000) => 0xfc00          // log(+0.0) = -INF
V_LOG_F16(0x3c00) => 0x0000          // log(+1.0) = 0
V_LOG_F16(0x7c00) => 0x7c00          // log(+INF) = +INF

name: V_EXP_F16
description: Calculate 2 raised to the power of the half-precision float input and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
0.51ULP accuracy, denormals are supported.

Functional examples:

V_EXP_F16(0xfc00) => 0x0000          // exp(-INF) = 0
V_EXP_F16(0x8000) => 0x3c00          // exp(-0.0) = 1
V_EXP_F16(0x7c00) => 0x7c00          // exp(+INF) = +INF

name: V_FREXP_MANT_F16
description: Extract the binary significand, or mantissa, of an FP16 float input and store the result as an FP16 float into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f16) == +INF) || (64'F(S0.f16) == -INF) || isNAN(64'F(S0.f16))) then
        D0.f16 = S0.f16
  else
        D0.f16 = mantissa(S0.f16)
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f16 = significand * (2 ** exponent). Result range is in (-1.0,-0.5][0.5,1.0)
in normal cases. See also V_FREXP_EXP_I_F16, which returns integer exponent. See the C library function
frexp() for more information.

name: V_FREXP_EXP_I16_F16
description: Extract the exponent of an FP16 float input and store the result as a signed 16-bit integer into a vector register.
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f16) == +INF) || (64'F(S0.f16) == -INF) || isNAN(64'F(S0.f16))) then
        D0.i16 = 16'0
  else
        D0.i16 = 16'I(exponent(S0.f16) - 15 + 1)
  endif
notes:
This operation satisfies the invariant S0.f16 = significand * (2 ** exponent). See also V_FREXP_MANT_F16,
which returns the significand. See the C library function frexp() for more information.

name: V_FLOOR_F16
description: Round the half-precision float input down to previous integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.

name: V_CEIL_F16
description: Round the half-precision float input up to next integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.

name: V_TRUNC_F16
description: Compute the integer part of an FP16 float input with round-toward-zero semantics and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f16 = trunc(S0.f16)

name: V_RNDNE_F16
description: Round the half-precision float input to the nearest even integer and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
pseudocode:
D0.f16 = floor(S0.f16 + 16'0.5);
  if (isEven(64'F(floor(S0.f16))) && (fract(S0.f16) == 16'0.5)) then
      D0.f16 -= 16'1.0
  endif

name: V_FRACT_F16
description: Compute the fractional portion of an FP16 float input and store the result in floating point format into a vector register.
notes:
0.5ULP accuracy, denormals are accepted.

This is intended to comply with the DX specification of fract where the function behaves like an extension of
integer modulus; be aware this may differ from how fract() is defined in other domains. For example: fract(-
1.2) = 0.8 in DX.

name: V_SIN_F16
description: Calculate the trigonometric sine of an FP16 float value using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register. The operand is calculated by scaling the vector input by 2 PI.
notes:
Denormals are supported. Full range input is supported.

Functional examples:

V_SIN_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00          // sin(-INF) = NAN
V_SIN_F16(0xfbff) => 0x0000          // Most negative finite FP16
V_SIN_F16(0x8000) => 0x8000          // sin(-0.0) = -0
V_SIN_F16(0x3400) => 0x3c00          // sin(0.25) = 1
V_SIN_F16(0x7bff) => 0x0000          // Most positive finite FP16
V_SIN_F16(0x7c00) => 0xfe00          // sin(+INF) = NAN

name: V_COS_F16
description: Calculate the trigonometric cosine of an FP16 float value using IEEE rules and store the result into a vector register. The operand is calculated by scaling the vector input by 2 PI.
notes:
Denormals are supported. Full range input is supported.

Functional examples:

V_COS_F16(0xfc00) => 0xfe00          // cos(-INF) = NAN
V_COS_F16(0xfbff) => 0x3c00          // Most negative finite FP16
V_COS_F16(0x8000) => 0x3c00          // cos(-0.0) = 1
V_COS_F16(0x3400) => 0x0000          // cos(0.25) = 0

V_COS_F16(0x7bff) => 0x3c00          // Most positive finite FP16
V_COS_F16(0x7c00) => 0xfe00          // cos(+INF) = NAN

name: V_SAT_PK_U8_I16
description: Given two 16-bit unsigned integer inputs, saturate each input over an 8-bit unsigned range, pack the resulting values into a 16-bit word and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
SAT8 = lambda(n) (
        if n.i <= 0 then
            return 8'0U
        elsif n >= 16'I(0xff) then
            return 8'255U
        else
            return n[7 : 0].u8
        endif);
  D0.b16 = { SAT8(S0[31 : 16].i16), SAT8(S0[15 : 0].i16) }
notes: Used for 4x16bit data packed as 4x8bit data.

name: V_CVT_NORM_I16_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to a signed normalized short and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i16 = f16_to_snorm(S0.f16)
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, supports rounding, exception flags and saturation, denormals are supported.

name: V_CVT_NORM_U16_F16
description: Convert from an FP16 float input to an unsigned normalized short and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u16 = f16_to_unorm(S0.f16)
notes: 0.5ULP accuracy, supports rounding, exception flags and saturation, denormals are supported.

name: V_NOT_B16
description: Calculate bitwise negation on a vector input and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_CVT_I32_I16
description: Convert from an 16-bit signed integer to a 32-bit signed integer, sign extending as needed.
pseudocode: D0.i = 32'I(signext(S0.i16))
notes: To convert in the other direction (from 32-bit to 16-bit integer) use V_MOV_B16.

name: V_CVT_U32_U16
description: Convert from an 16-bit unsigned integer to a 32-bit unsigned integer, zero extending as needed.
notes: To convert in the other direction (from 32-bit to 16-bit integer) use V_MOV_B16.

name: V_CNDMASK_B32
description: Copy data from one of two inputs based on the vector condition code and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u = VCC.u64[laneId] ? S1.u : S0.u
notes:
In VOP3 the VCC source may be a scalar GPR specified in S2.

Floating-point modifiers are valid for this instruction if S0 and S1 are 32-bit floating point values. This

instruction is suitable for negating or taking the absolute value of a floating-point value.

name: V_ADD_F32
description: Add two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S0.f + S1.f
notes: 0.5ULP precision, denormals are supported.

name: V_SUB_F32
description: Subtract the second floating point input from the first input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S0.f - S1.f
notes: 0.5ULP precision, denormals are supported.

name: V_SUBREV_F32
description: Subtract the first floating point input from the second input and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S1.f - S0.f
notes: 0.5ULP precision, denormals are supported.

name: V_FMAC_DX9_ZERO_F32
description: Multiply two single-precision values and accumulate the result with the destination. Follows DX9 rules where 0.0 times anything produces 0.0 (this is not IEEE compliant).
pseudocode:
if ((64'F(S0.f) == 0.0) || (64'F(S1.f) == 0.0)) then
        // DX9 rules, 0.0 * x = 0.0
        D0.f = S2.f

  else
        D0.f = fma(S0.f, S1.f, D0.f)
  endif

name: V_MUL_DX9_ZERO_F32
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and store the result in a vector register. Follows DX9 rules where 0.0 times anything produces 0.0 (this differs from other APIs when the other input is infinity or NaN).

name: V_MUL_F32
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.f = S0.f * S1.f
notes: 0.5ULP precision, denormals are supported.

name: V_MUL_I32_I24
description: Multiply two signed 24 bit integer inputs and store the result as a signed 32 bit integer into a vector register.
notes:
This opcode is expected to be as efficient as basic single-precision opcodes since it utilizes the single-precision
floating point multiplier. See also V_MUL_HI_I32_I24.

name: V_MUL_HI_I32_I24
description: Multiply two signed 24 bit integer inputs and store the high 32 bits of the result as a signed 32 bit integer into a vector register.
notes: See also V_MUL_I32_I24.

name: V_MUL_U32_U24
description: Multiply two unsigned 24 bit integer inputs and store the result as a unsigned 32 bit integer into a vector register.
notes:
This opcode is expected to be as efficient as basic single-precision opcodes since it utilizes the single-precision
floating point multiplier. See also V_MUL_HI_U32_U24.

name: V_MUL_HI_U32_U24
description: Multiply two unsigned 24 bit integer inputs and store the high 32 bits of the result as a unsigned 32 bit integer into a vector register.
notes: See also V_MUL_U32_U24.

name: V_MIN_F32
description: Select the minimum of two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
LT_NEG_ZERO = lambda(a, b) (
        ((a < b) || ((64'F(abs(a)) == 0.0) && (64'F(abs(b)) == 0.0) && sign(a) && !sign(b))));
  // Version of comparison where -0.0 < +0.0, differs from IEEE
  if WAVE_MODE.IEEE then
        if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = 32'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f)))
        elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then

            D0.f = 32'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f)))
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = S0.f
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = S1.f
        elsif LT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f, S1.f) then
            // NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f = S0.f
        else
            D0.f = S1.f
        endif
  else
        if isNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = S0.f
        elsif isNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = S1.f
        elsif LT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f, S1.f) then
            // NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f = S0.f
        else
            D0.f = S1.f
        endif
  endif;
  // Inequalities in the above pseudocode behave differently from IEEE
  // when both inputs are +-0.
notes:
IEEE compliant. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags, saturation.

Denorm flushing for this operation is effectively controlled by the input denorm mode control: If input
denorm mode is disabling denorm, the internal result of a min/max operation cannot be a denorm value, so
output denorm mode is irrelevant. If input denorm mode is enabling denorm, the internal min/max result can
be a denorm and this operation outputs as a denorm regardless of output denorm mode.

name: V_MAX_F32
description: Select the maximum of two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
GT_NEG_ZERO = lambda(a, b) (
        ((a > b) || ((64'F(abs(a)) == 0.0) && (64'F(abs(b)) == 0.0) && !sign(a) && sign(b))));
  // Version of comparison where +0.0 > -0.0, differs from IEEE
  if WAVE_MODE.IEEE then
        if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = 32'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f)))
        elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = 32'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f)))
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = S0.f
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = S1.f
        elsif GT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f, S1.f) then
            // NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison

            D0.f = S0.f
        else
            D0.f = S1.f
        endif
  else
        if isNAN(64'F(S1.f)) then
            D0.f = S0.f
        elsif isNAN(64'F(S0.f)) then
            D0.f = S1.f
        elsif GT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f, S1.f) then
            // NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f = S0.f
        else
            D0.f = S1.f
        endif
  endif;
  // Inequalities in the above pseudocode behave differently from IEEE
  // when both inputs are +-0.
notes:
IEEE compliant. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags, saturation.

Denorm flushing for this operation is effectively controlled by the input denorm mode control: If input
denorm mode is disabling denorm, the internal result of a min/max operation cannot be a denorm value, so
output denorm mode is irrelevant. If input denorm mode is enabling denorm, the internal min/max result can
be a denorm and this operation outputs as a denorm regardless of output denorm mode.

name: V_MIN_I32
description: Select the minimum of two signed integers and store the selected value into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i = S0.i < S1.i ? S0.i : S1.i

name: V_MAX_I32
description: Select the maximum of two signed integers and store the selected value into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.i = S0.i >= S1.i ? S0.i : S1.i

name: V_MIN_U32
description: Select the minimum of two unsigned integers and store the selected value into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u = S0.u < S1.u ? S0.u : S1.u

name: V_MAX_U32
description: Select the maximum of two unsigned integers and store the selected value into a vector register.
pseudocode: D0.u = S0.u >= S1.u ? S0.u : S1.u

name: V_LSHLREV_B32
description: Given a shift count in the first vector input, calculate the logical shift left of the second vector input and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_LSHRREV_B32
description: Given a shift count in the first vector input, calculate the logical shift right of the second vector input and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_ASHRREV_I32
description: Given a shift count in the first vector input, calculate the arithmetic shift right (preserving sign bit) of the second vector input and store the result into a vector register.

name: V_AND_B32
description: Calculate bitwise AND on two vector inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_OR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise OR on two vector inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_XOR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise XOR on two vector inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_XNOR_B32
description: Calculate bitwise XNOR on two vector inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: Input and output modifiers not supported.

name: V_ADD_CO_CI_U32
description: Add two unsigned inputs and a bit from a carry-in mask, store the result into a vector register and store the carry-out mask into a scalar register.
notes:
In VOP3 the VCC destination may be an arbitrary SGPR-pair, and the VCC source comes from the SGPR-pair at
S2.u.

Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_SUB_CO_CI_U32
description: Subtract the second unsigned input from the first input, subtract a bit from the carry-in mask, store the result into a vector register and store the carry-out mask to a scalar register.
notes:
In VOP3 the VCC destination may be an arbitrary SGPR-pair, and the VCC source comes from the SGPR-pair at
S2.u.

Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_SUBREV_CO_CI_U32
description: Subtract the first unsigned input from the second input, subtract a bit from the carry-in mask, store the result into a vector register and store the carry-out mask to a scalar register.
notes:
In VOP3 the VCC destination may be an arbitrary SGPR-pair, and the VCC source comes from the SGPR-pair at
S2.u.

Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_ADD_NC_U32
description: Add two unsigned inputs and store the result into a vector register. No carry-in or carry-out support.
pseudocode: D0.u = S0.u + S1.u
notes: Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_SUB_NC_U32
description: Subtract the second unsigned input from the first input and store the result into a vector register. No carry-in or carry-out support.
pseudocode: D0.u = S0.u - S1.u
notes: Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_SUBREV_NC_U32
description: Subtract the first unsigned input from the second input and store the result into a vector register. No carry-in or carry-out support.
pseudocode: D0.u = S1.u - S0.u
notes: Supports saturation (unsigned 32-bit integer domain).

name: V_FMAC_F32
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and accumulate the result into the destination register using fused multiply- add.
pseudocode: D0.f = fma(S0.f, S1.f, D0.f)

name: V_CVT_PK_RTZ_F16_F32
description: Convert two single-precision float inputs into a packed FP16 result with round toward zero semantics (ignore the current rounding mode), and store the result into a vector register.
notes:
This opcode is intended for use with 16-bit compressed exports. See V_CVT_F16_F32 for a version that respects
the current rounding mode.

name: V_ADD_F16
description: Add two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_SUB_F16
description: Subtract the second floating point input from the first input and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_SUBREV_F16
description: Subtract the first floating point input from the second input and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_MUL_F16
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_FMAC_F16
description: Multiply two floating point inputs and accumulate the result into the destination register using fused multiply- add.
notes: 0.5ULP precision. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags and saturation.

name: V_MAX_F16
description: Select the maximum of two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
GT_NEG_ZERO = lambda(a, b) (
        ((a > b) || ((64'F(abs(a)) == 0.0) && (64'F(abs(b)) == 0.0) && !sign(a) && sign(b))));
  // Version of comparison where +0.0 > -0.0, differs from IEEE
  if WAVE_MODE.IEEE then
        if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)))
        elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)))
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        elsif GT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f16, S1.f16) then
            // NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        else
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        endif
  else
        if isNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then

            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        elsif isNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        elsif GT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f16, S1.f16) then
            // NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        else
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        endif
  endif;
  // Inequalities in the above pseudocode behave differently from IEEE
  // when both inputs are +-0.
notes:
IEEE compliant. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags, saturation.

Denorm flushing for this operation is effectively controlled by the input denorm mode control: If input
denorm mode is disabling denorm, the internal result of a min/max operation cannot be a denorm value, so
output denorm mode is irrelevant. If input denorm mode is enabling denorm, the internal min/max result can
be a denorm and this operation outputs as a denorm regardless of output denorm mode.

name: V_MIN_F16
description: Select the minimum of two floating point inputs and store the result into a vector register.
pseudocode:
LT_NEG_ZERO = lambda(a, b) (
        ((a < b) || ((64'F(abs(a)) == 0.0) && (64'F(abs(b)) == 0.0) && sign(a) && !sign(b))));
  // Version of comparison where -0.0 < +0.0, differs from IEEE
  if WAVE_MODE.IEEE then
        if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)))
        elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)))
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        elsif LT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f16, S1.f16) then
            // NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        else
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        endif
  else
        if isNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        elsif isNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        elsif LT_NEG_ZERO(S0.f16, S1.f16) then
            // NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
            D0.f16 = S0.f16
        else

            D0.f16 = S1.f16
        endif
  endif;
  // Inequalities in the above pseudocode behave differently from IEEE
  // when both inputs are +-0.
notes:
IEEE compliant. Supports denormals, round mode, exception flags, saturation.
